Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 6-Day Chimpanzee & Gorilla Tracking Uganda
touren.desc: Discover Uganda’s wildlife with expert guides on this unforgettable 6-day African safari. Track chimpanzees, encounter mountain gorillas in the Bwindi Impenetrable Forest, and explore stunning landscapes. Book your adventure today!
highlights: Witness over 13 species of primates, including rare monkeys and forest elephants
Spend a magical hour with mountain gorillas in Bwindi Impenetrable Forest, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its biodiversity
Walk through papyrus-lined trails and spot colourful birds, butterflies, and primates
Follow knowledgeable guides who enrich your experience with insights into local wildlife, flora, and conservation efforts
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Uganda
On your arrival at Entebbe International Airport you will be met by your Manya Africa Tours guide who will greet you and transfer you to your hotel. Depending on traffic this will take between 15 and 40 minutes.
Overnight at Airport Guesthouse (B&B)/ at Karibu Guesthouse

Day 2: Depart for Fort Portal – Kibale National Park
This morning you will depart for Fort Portal and explore the area around the base of the Rwenzori Mountains (with an optional visit to the world-famous Rwenzori Mountain Sculpture Foundation).
Later, you’ll drive to Kibale Forest National Park, in Western Uganda, an incredibly diverse forest that is home to over 13 species of primates including chimpanzee, red colobus monkey, the rare L’Hoest monkey, blue monkey, grey mangabey, black faced vervet monkeys, baboons, pottos and bushbabies as well as various forest bird species.
Overnight at Kibale Forest Camp (HB)/ Primate Lodge Luxury cottage (HB)

Day 3: Chimpanzee Tracking in the Jungle (Kibale National Park)
After breakfast, we report to the Ranger Headquarters of Kibale Forest National Park at 8 AM to set out to find the chimpanzees living there. It’s great fun to watch these active apes play and romp in the fruit trees.
The experienced ranger will introduce you to the various members of the chimpanzee family and show you many more inhabitants of the forest. As you walk through the forest on small trails, you’ll gain insight into how the locals use the woodland plants to combat some diseases, amongst other uses.
Even the elusive forest elephant, which is smaller and bears more fur than its brother from the savannah, moves in the Kibale Forest. Sometimes even large mammals such as buffalo, large forest pigs and various antelope species can be found.
In the afternoon, you’ll visit the neighbouring Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary. Cleverly constructed walking paths guide you through the papyrus that grows above your head. With over 130 species of birds, colourful butterflies, eight species of primates and the rare semi-aquatic Sitatunga antelope, this area is a showcase of responsible tourism.
Overnight at Kibale Forest Camp (HB)/ Primate Lodge Luxury cottage (FB)

Day 4: Depart for Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
Bwindi Impenetrable National Park is regarded as a UNESCO-Natural-World- Heritage and is one of the richest biological and botanical regions in the world. With its south-west position at the edge of the western Rift-Valley, this forest covers 331 km2. The treasures of Bwindi are the approx. 400 wild Mountain gorillas. They contribute to about half of the world population of these gentle human
Apes.
You’ll arrive in Buhoma in the afternoon, where the adventure continues. If you’re up to it, take a stroll around the area—there’s plenty to explore. You might even find something special in one of the local art and handicraft shops.
Overnight Haven Bwindi (HB)/ Mahogany Springs (FB)

Day 5: In Touch With the Gorillas in Bwindi
Get up very early for your gorilla tracking and have a well-balanced breakfast in preparation for the day. After the registration at the Uganda Wildlife Authority Ranger Station and a short briefing, your incredible gorilla tracking starts.
Once you’ve located the gorilla family you’ll be asked to leave all your belongings behind and slowly approach these gentle giants with only your camera in hand. The guide interprets the behaviour of the animals for you. Females and their young ones, young males and even the dominant silverback can be seen feeding, playing and interacting with each other or just relaxing! You will never forget this experience with the gorillas. Rest assured, they’ll be as curious to see you as you are to see them. You have exactly one hour with the gorillas before you return to the base station.
After the exciting encounter with the gorillas you will return to the village. In the afternoon, you’ll have the opportunity to see the Batwa, the indigenous people of the forest.
Overnight at Haven Bwindi (FB)/ Mahogany Springs (FB)

Day 6: Transfer back to Kampala with a stop at the Equator
After breakfast, you will journey back towards Entebbe International Airport, stopping for a picnic lunch and a fun photo opportunity at the Equator.
A dayroom will be organised for you to shower and change in Entebbe. After a farewell dinner, your Manya Africa Tours guide will transfer you to Entebbe International Airport for your outbound flight.
Please note that check-in for international flights should be done two hours prior to flight departure. The departures area of the airport has a small restaurant, several duty-free shops and a seating area.
Note: It is also possible to fly back from Bwindi to Entebbe. Please see the optional flight price in the Rates section.
Dayroom at Airport Guesthouse (HB)/ Karibu Guesthouse

I hope we can come back to Uganda one day for another visit, and we will go to Manya and Saadi again.

Sabine H
26 Nov 2025
(opens in a new tab)
Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 8-Day Jewels of Uganda LUXURY Safari
touren.desc: Experience an exclusive 8-day luxury Uganda safari, featuring gorilla tracking, chimpanzee encounters, and an unforgettable boat safari on the Kazinga Channel. Book your adventure now!
highlights: Witness mountain gorillas in their natural habitat, an awe-inspiring, once-in-a-lifetime experience
Cruise the wildlife-rich waters of the Kazinga Channel to spot elephants, hippos, crocodiles, and hundreds of bird species up close
Track chimpanzees in Kibale Forest
Explore Queen Elizabeth National Park on a scenic game drive in search of lions, leopards, and diverse wildlife
Relax in exquisite lodges with panoramic views and top-tier amenities amidst Uganda’s stunning landscapes
Experience the enchanting Bigodi Wetlands Sanctuary
were cleverly constructed walking paths lead you through lush papyrus swamps
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival to the Pearl of Africa
On your arrival at Entebbe International Airport you will be met by your Manya Africa Tours guide who will greet you and transfer you to your hotel. This will take between 15 to 40 Minutes depending on traffic.
Overnight at Lake Victoria Serena (B&B)

Day 2: Depart for Kibale Forest
This morning we will drive to Fort Portal and explore the area around the base of the Rwenzori Mountains (with an optional visit to the world-famous Rwenzori Mountain Sculpture Foundation). It is also possible to pay extra and fly to Kasese via Aerolink. You can see the pricing for this flight under the Rates section.
Later, we’ll drive to Kibale Forest National Park, in Western Uganda. Kibale Forest National Park, a multi-faceted forest of 795 square kilometres, is home to over 335 species of birds (some very rare and only found here), and 13 species of primates including chimpanzees, red colobus monkey, the rare L’Hoest monkey, blue monkey, grey mangabey, black faced vervet monkeys, baboons, pottos and bushbabies.
Cleverly constructed walking paths lead you through Papyrus that towers above your head in Bigodi Wetlands Sanctuary, a few minutes’ drive from Kibale Forest. With over 130 bird species, multicoloured butterflies, eight different types of primates and the rare, semi-aquatic sitatunga (antelope), this is community-based tourism at its best.
Set against the stunning backdrop of Lake Kyaninga and the Rwenzori Mountains beyond, Kyaninga Lodge is the spectacular result of one man’s vision and six years of unwavering commitment to creating an experience of Africa’s beauty unlike anything else.
You can experience the beauty and romance of this unexplored region while staying in the height of luxury and elegance. Nine exclusive cottages made from hand carved logs, superb dining, and a wealth of adventures await.
Overnight Kyaninga/Primate Safari Lodge (FB)

Day 3: Chimpanzee-Tracking in the Jungle (Kibale Forest National Park)
After breakfast, we report to the Ranger Headquarters of the Kibale Forest National Park at 8 AM to set out to find the chimpanzees living there. It’s great fun to watch these active apes play and roam in the fruit trees.
The experienced ranger will introduce you to the various family members of the chimpanzee family and show you many more inhabitants of the forest. As you walk through the forest on small trails, you will be told how the locals use the woodland plants to combat some diseases, amongst other uses. Even the elusive forest elephant, which is smaller and bears more fur than its brother from the savannah, moves in the Kibale Forest. Sometimes even large mammals such as buffalo, large forest pigs and various antelope species can be admired.
In the afternoon, we will visit the neighbouring Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary. Cleverly constructed walking paths guide you through the papyrus that grows above your heads. With over 130 species of birds, colourful butterflies, eight species of primates and the rare semi-aquatic sitatunga antelope, this area is a showcase of responsible tourism.
Overnight Kyaninga/Primate Safari Lodge (FB)

Day 4: Drive to Queen Elizabeth National Park
Your Guide will drive you through Kasese towards Queen Elizabeth National Park, which is at the foot of the Rift Valley. From time to time you’ll be able to observe the peaks of the Rwenzori Mountains on the horizon. The landscape of northern Queen Elizabeth consists of several dozen craters. With more than 600 species of birds, Queen Elizabeth National Park offers even greater diversity than other national parks in Uganda. On the way to the lodge you can observe a large variety of wildlife such as elephants. Keep your eyes open for the hard-to-find leopards! Today’s accommodation is high above the Kichwamba Ridge and offers breathtaking views over the bottom of the Rift Valley. While the foothills of the Rwenzoris are in front of you, the eight mighty Virunga volcanoes rise to the other side.
Overnight at Katara Lodge or Mweya Lodge which both offer panoramic views of the whole park (FB)

Day 5: Boat safari on the Kazinga Channel
Start your day with an early morning game drive. You can ask the hotel staff to prepare a boxed breakfast which you can enjoy enroute. A popular route for the game drive is the Kasenyi sector, well-known for its lions that prey on Queen Elizabeth’s large population of Uganda Kob (antelope). If you are very lucky, you may spot a Giant Forest Hog, Africa’s largest pig. You should also keep your eyes peeled to spot an elusive leopard.
Optional: Enjoy a bird’s-eye view over the Savannah via a hot balloon ride. You can find the price for this activity in the Rates section.
After your game drive, you will head back to the lodge for a few hours’ relaxation by the pool and lunch.
In the afternoon, you’ll embark on a water safari by boat, on the Kazinga Channel at Mweya that connects the two lakes of Edward and George. This is likely to be a big highlight of your trip! The expert Uganda Wildlife Authority guides will identify many of the Channel’s 100 bird species of which you are likely to see African Skimmers, Great White Pelicans, Spoonbill and a few Bee-eater species. Hippos wallow at the edge of the water, huge Nile crocodiles bask in the sun and elephants come down to drink.
The Kazinga Channel boat ride is a brilliant opportunity to take some close-up wildlife shots.
Overnight Mweya/Katara Lodge (FB)
DAY 6: In Search of the tree climbing lions – Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
Today, we’ll drive to the Bwindi Impenetrable Forest and on the way take a short game drive through Ishasha, the southern sector of Queen Elizabeth National Park. On the vast plains of the Ishasha you will find a famous inhabitant, the tree-climbing lion. A sighting is not guaranteed, however, in the large, low-hanging fig trees of Ishasha there are far better chances to see a lion, in the rest of Africa.
Bwindi Impenetrable National Park is regarded as a UNESCO-Natural-World- Heritage and is one of the richest biological and botanical regions in the world. With its south-west position at the edge of the western Rift-Valley, this forest covers 331 km2. The treasures of Bwindi are the approx. 400 wild mountain gorillas. They contribute to about half of the world population of these gentle human apes.
After a few hours car journey, you can take a relaxing afternoon walk in the village of Buhoma, your home in Bwindi, and enjoy a visit to some of the many craft markets. Or you can visit the Batwa tribe who live in the middle of the forest. Your current accommodation is elevated and from there you can overlook the breathtakingly beautiful Bwindi Impenetrable Forest National Park.
Mahogany Springs offers privacy and serenity blended with optimal comfort in a spectacular setting. With some of the most luxurious accommodation in East Africa the feeling that you will experience when staying here is beyond compare.
Overnight Mahogany Springs Lodge (FB)

Day 7: In touch with the Gorillas (Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Get up very early for your gorilla tracking and have a well-balanced breakfast in preparation for the day. After the registration at the Uganda Wildlife Authority Ranger Station and a short briefing, your incredible gorilla tracking starts.
Once you’ve located the gorilla family, you’ll be asked to leave all your belongings behind and slowly approach these gentle giants with only your camera in hand. The guide interprets the behaviour of the animals for you. Females and their young ones, young males and even the dominant silverback can be seen feeding, playing and interacting with each other or just relaxing! You will never forget this experience with the gorillas. Rest assured, they are as curious to see you as you are them. You have exactly one hour with the gorillas before you return to the base station.
After the exciting encounter with the gorillas you will return to the village. In the afternoon, you’ll have the opportunity to see the Batwa, the indigenous people of the forest.
Overnight Mahogany Springs Lodge (FB)

Day 8: Depart for Entebbe
After breakfast, we start our journey back towards Entebbe. We’ll stop for a picnic lunch and a fun photo opportunity at the Equator. You can also fly from Kihihi to Entebbe via Aerolink. See the pricing for this optional flight in the Rates section.
A dayroom will be organised for you to shower and change in Entebbe. After a farewell dinner, your Manya Africa Tours guide will transfer you to Entebbe International Airport for your outbound flight.
Please note that check-in for international flights should be done two hours prior to flight departure. The departures area of the airport has a small restaurant, several duty-free shops and a seating area.
Dayroom at Lake Victoria Serena (FB)
Before Booking, Please Read the Following Carefully
Prices are valid for 2026.
The itinerary mentioned for this tour is an example which can be tailored to your wishes.
The lodges and hotels are subject to availability at the time of booking. In the event that a property is unavailable, we will be pleased to offer a suitable alternative of a similar range, quality, and service standard to ensure your comfort and satisfaction.
The Gorilla Tracking included in this safari depends on the availability of gorilla trekking permits at the time of booking. These permits are strictly limited and sold out in advance, especially during peak seasons of Christmas and school holidays. We therefore strongly recommend booking early.
The safari indicated here is essentially an offer. Changes in the course of the journey, the duration of your tour, the number of travellers, as well as the choice of accommodation are subject to availability, although we always endeavour to stick as closely as possible to this programme. For most itineraries, we can offer luxury, mid-range or budget versions of this safari upon request.
Sometimes it is not possible to adhere to the travel plan as described in this itinerary, due to weather and road conditions, authority regulations, organisational requirements, etc. Occasionally, the planned route of the tour has to change according to the situation on the ground. We must therefore explicitly refuse liability for adherence to the journey plan.

This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.

touren.name: 8-Day North East Uganda Safari
touren.desc: Extraordinary multi-day adventure through Uganda's stunning landscapes, featuring guided hikes, cultural immersion, and thrilling game drives. Enjoy a Sipi Falls hike, and explore Mt. Moroto, and Kidepo Valley National Park. Book now for an all-inclusive experience!
highlights: Trek through the lush trails of Sipi Falls and take in the spectacular views of three cascading waterfalls and the surrounding volcanic landscape
Gain insight into the traditions of the Karamojong people with an authentic village tour and the unique experience of spending a night in a traditional kraal, sleeping under the expansive African sky
Ascend the scenic slopes of Mt. Moroto for breathtaking vistas and a chance to connect with the vibrant culture of Uganda’s highland communities
Embark on thrilling game drives across the vast savannahs of Kidepo, a pristine wilderness teeming with diverse wildlife, including species found nowhere else in Uganda
Roast your own coffee at the source of Arabica with a hands-on experience using traditional methods
Venture into one of Africa’s most remote and untouched national parks, Kidepo Valley, where you’ll encounter Uganda’s rarest wildlife in an unspoiled savannah landscape
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival
On your arrival at Entebbe International Airport you will be met by your Manya Africa Tours guide who will greet you and transfer you to your hotel. Depending on traffic this will take between 15 and 40 minutes.
Overnight at The Boma (no meals included)

Day 2: Sipi Falls
In the early morning we’ll drive to the east to Sipi Falls, where you will not only find countless bird species, but also a large volcanic base.
At Sipi Falls, you’ll find the perfect opportunity to undertake adventurous activities such as hiking, bike tours and fishing. You can choose to spend the day exploring the area on foot and bask in the breathtaking sunsets from the Sipi waterfalls. You can also take a refreshing swim in one of the pools under the waterfalls, or you can roast your own coffee and enjoy a nice cup of Arabica coffee. The coffee roasting tour and Sipi Falls hike is included in this tour package, and you’ll have the option of visiting two or three waterfalls. At the end of the day you will stay in a traditional lodge.
Overnight at Rafiki Lodge
All meals included
Distance: 300km, 7 hours

Day 3: Visit to Karamojong
A unique experience awaits you today. We’ll drive northeast to Moroto for about 5 hours for a visit to Karamojong.
You have a lot to experience during your stay here. The mountain ranges are quietly waiting to be discovered by adventurous travellers. The mountains are an exciting alternative to the otherwise strenuous climbs in Uganda with many attractions in the surrounding areas and a mild climate that everyone can enjoy. No experience or climbing equipment is required.
In the late afternoon we embark on a Karamojong Cultural Tour which consists of a traditional village tour and a night in the kraal under the African starry sky. This is an experience you will remember long after your visit!
2 nights’ stay at Kara Tunga Safari camp OR 1 night stay in the Kral of the Karamojong (if you dare), and 1 overnight stay at the safari camp
All meals included
Distance: 150km, 5 hours

Day 4: Hike Mt Moroto – Half day hike of Mt Moroto is included
The Mountains of Karamoja are an exciting alternative to the more strenuous climbs in East Africa with many of the same attractions, a milder climate, lower elevation and no special equipment required. Our expeditions are a mix of rich encounters with Uganda’s highland people, breathtaking views, unique sights along the trails and unique places where you can enjoy the sunset, the stars and sunrise over the planes of Karamoja.
Day 5 and 6: Drive to the most beautiful National Park of Uganda – Kidepo National Park
After a leisurely breakfast we’ll continue to Kidepo National Park. The Kidepo Valley National Park, embedded in the wild border region of Uganda, is apparently the most isolated national park in northern Uganda. However, it is still considered one of the best virgin places that any animal watcher can ever visit in Uganda. This pristine national park is undoubtedly one of the best wilderness areas in Africa and is home to a few endemic animal species that cannot be found in any other Ugandan national park. Kidepo is located in the middle of a savannah landscape, right next to some mountain ranges.
The Kidepo National Park is a real gem with a huge wealth of wildlife. It is still a true undiscovered, unknown pearl of tourism in Uganda! Kidepo Valley National Park was converted into a national park in 1962 and is currently home to over 75 mammal and 470 bird species. All of this falls into a prime location for game drives, for which we will have plenty of time in the next 2 days.
2 nights at Kidepo Savannah Lodge
All meals included
Distance: 250km, 6 hours

Day 7: Break in Gulu
After breakfast, prepare to check out of the lodge and go for your last game drive in this true wilderness. Every day presents a different sight and you can expect to increase your game count. Transfer to Gulu where you can enjoy lunch (own expenditure) and spend the rest of the day relaxing
Dinner and overnight at African Roots
Breakfast and dinner included
Distance: 200km, 4 hours

Day 8: Back to Entebbe
Start the day with an early breakfast before embarking on a leisurely drive towards Kampala, enjoying a packed lunch along the way. If time permits, we can make a stop at the Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary (additional $50 per person). This incredible sanctuary is the only spot in Uganda where you can see rhinos, thanks to a successful reintroduction program that has bred over a dozen white rhinos.
We’ll arrive in Entebbe by late afternoon or evening, where you’ll either be transferred to the airport for your onward flight or dropped off at your hotel in Entebbe or Kampala.
Breakfast and packed lunch
Distance: 350 km, 6 hours

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 8-Day Uganda Safari with Authentic Homestays
touren.desc: Embark on an unforgettable Ugandan safari, exploring Murchison Falls, chimpanzee treks, and cultural homestays. Discover wildlife, waterfalls, and local traditions on this adventure of a lifetime!
highlights: Encounter Uganda’s only white rhinos in their natural habitat at the Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary
Experience the power of the Nile as it thunders through a narrow gorge at Murchison Falls
Go on thrilling game drives to spot lions, elephants, leopards, and more in iconic national parks
Enjoy a scenic Kazinga Channel boat cruise with close-up views of hippos, elephants, and birds
Trek through Kibale Forest to observe chimpanzees and other primates
Immerse yourself in Ugandan culture at welcoming community-run homestays
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Entebbe
Your adventure begins the moment you land! Your friendly guide will greet you at the airport and take you to his home, where you’ll be treated to a warm welcome dinner. Enjoy a cozy introduction to Uganda while getting to know your guide and his family, setting the perfect tone for the unforgettable journey ahead.
Overnight either at the guides home or at African Roots (dinner included depending on arrival time)

Day 2: Drive to Murchison Falls National Park
Today you’ll drive to Murchison Falls National Park, a protected area of over 4000 km2. If time permits, you will stop at Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary, a reintroduction program that has successfully bred over a dozen white rhinos and is the only place in Uganda where you can see rhinos. These good-natured animals can best be observed on an early game drive.
Murchison Falls National Park has 76 mammal species and 451 bird species. The Nile River winds through the park, drawing in Nile crocodiles, hippos, and the elusive Sitatunga antelope, a unique creature that thrives in both water and land. Lions, leopards, elephants, buffalos, the Rothschild giraffe, warthogs, the big forest pig, Uganda kob, topi and water buffalos are just a few of the great mammals you will see. If time permits, hike to the top of the falls and feel the power of the Nile River, which narrows under your feet from half a kilometre to only 7 m and plunges down 45 m.
Today, your accommodation awaits at Murchison River Lodge, an eco-lodge which is perfectly situated at the Nile. The safari thatched tents are the ideal accommodation to enjoy the sounds of nature.
Overnight at Murchison River Lodge (FB)

Day 3: Boat ride to the bottom of the falls
Your morning starts with an early game drive. You may wish to have your breakfast packed to take along with you, or you can have a full breakfast when you get back to the lodge. Admire the wildlife and bird life of Murchison Falls National Park and take advantage of asking your guide as many questions as you like on the way.
After the game drive, you will return to the lodge for lunch and an hour of relaxation by the pool before going on what will likely be one of the highlights of this safari – a scenic boat trip to the bottom of the Murchison Falls. There are excellent photo opportunities with lots of elephants, hippos, Nile crocodiles and buffalos sunbathing on the banks or looking for food.
Overnight at Murchison River Lodge (FB)

Day 4: Drive to Kibale Forest National Park
Today, your guide will drive you through Hoima to the breathtaking Kibale Forest, a lush expanse covering 795 square kilometres. This incredibly rich ecosystem is home to over 335 bird species—some of which are rare and found only here—and 13 species of primates, including chimpanzees, Red Colobus Monkeys, the elusive L’Hoest’s Monkey, Blue Monkeys, Gray-cheeked Mangabeys, Black-faced Vervet Monkeys, baboons, pottos, and bush babies.
A special experience awaits you today as you stay at a traditional homestay run by John Tinka, situated close to Kibale National Park and the Bigodi Wetlands. John’s deep love for tourism and nature conservation has shaped his life and provided a bright future for his family. After retiring from the Uganda Wildlife Authority, he dedicated himself to community tourism by creating an amazing project that supports both conservation and local livelihoods. At his welcoming “home away from home,” we’ll immerse ourselves in local traditions and enjoy a variety of authentic, delicious dishes.
Overnight at Tinka Johns Homestay in Bigodi (FB)

Day 5: Chimpanzee tracking
After breakfast, head to the Ranger Headquarters at Kibale Forest National Park by 8 am for an exciting chimpanzee trek.
These lively primates, our closest relatives, are a joy to watch as they play and interact in the fruit trees. Your experienced ranger will introduce you to the different members of the chimpanzee family, as well as the diverse bird species and plant life in the forest.
The forest trails are generally easy to navigate, and you’ll always be accompanied by knowledgeable rangers who will share the fascinating uses of various forest plants. Keep an eye out for the elusive forest elephants, which are smaller, more fur-bearing, and harder to spot than their savannah brothers. Depending on the season, you might also encounter large mammals like buffalo, giant forest pigs, and various antelope species.
Bird enthusiasts will be thrilled with the chance to spot some of the 335 bird species in the area. Look out for the Black and White Colobus Monkey, Red-tailed Monkey, and Gray-cheeked Mangabey. Butterfly and plant lovers will also be amazed by the extensive variety of rare and native species.
At noon, we’ll return to John Tinka’s homestay, where he and his sons will take us on a culinary journey through western Uganda, sharing insights into the unique aspects of their culture.
In the afternoon, visit the nearby Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary. Walk along well-designed paths that lead you through towering papyrus in this remarkable wetland. With over 130 bird species, colourful butterflies, eight species of primates, and the rare semi-aquatic Sitatunga antelope, this sanctuary is a shining example of community-based tourism.
Overnight at Tinka Johns Homestay (FB)

Day 6: Departure to Queen Elizabeth National Park
Today you will journey to an Elephant Home campsite. Your driver will take you through Kasese, heading toward Queen Elizabeth National Park, nestled at the base of the Rift Valley with the stunning (and sometimes visible) peaks of the Rwenzori Mountains as a backdrop.
The northern part of Queen Elizabeth National Park is dotted with craters scattered among rolling hills. The park boasts over 600 bird species—more than any other national park in Uganda.
As we journey to our accommodation, keep an eye out for a variety of wildlife, especially elephants and Africa’s largest pig, the giant forest hog. If you’re lucky, you might even spot the elusive leopard!
Before settling into camp, we’ll visit the Rubona Honey Project to learn about the rare bees, their hives, and the honey-making process.
At the Elephant Home campsite, we encourage responsible travel in all our activities. Wildlife visits within the community offer sustainable opportunities for locals through tourism. The Elephant Home engages community members in tourism, leveraging the proximity to Queen Elizabeth National Park and its wildlife. By using tourism as a catalyst for community development and environmental conservation, we aim to shift local attitudes toward wildlife—especially elephants, which often threaten community gardens. The revenue from tourism supports the local population through skill-building, markets for their products, educational sponsorships, and other development initiatives.
Overnight at Elephant Home (FB)

Day 7: Game drive and boat safari on Kazinga Channel
Start your day early with your 6 o’clock coffee or tea alarm call, ready for your early game drive. Make sure you request the hotel staff to pack your breakfast this morning, to take with you. A very well known game drive route is the Kasenyi sector, famous for its lions that hunt the large stocks of Uganda Kob antelopes that mate here. Elephants, antilopes, warthogs, baboons and sometimes hyenas can be seen. If you are very lucky, you will see the big forest pig, Africa’s largest pig. Keep your eyes peeled for the elusive leopard!
In the afternoon, embark on a thrilling boat safari along the Kazinga Channel, which links Lake Edward and Lake George. This experience is sure to be one of the highlights of your trip! The Kazinga Channel lies at the heart of Queen Elizabeth National Park, where you’ll have the chance to see large mammals like hippos, elephants, and buffaloes as they gather along the lush, fertile banks.
The Uganda Wildlife Authority’s expert guide will identify many of the 100 bird species some of which include the African Skimmer, Great White Pelican, Spoonbill and a number of Bee Eater species. Hippos swim on the water’s edge, huge Nile crocodiles bask in the sun and elephants come down to drink. The Kazinga Channel boat trip is a brilliant opportunity to take some great close-up photos. After this we will drive more to the Southern Sector of Queen Elizabeth and stay overnight at a homestead 1km away from boundaries of the Ishasha Sector.
Overnight at Agatha’s Homestead or if you prefer no shifting in between second night at Elephant Home (FB)

Day 8: A stop at the Equator
Early in the morning, we’ll set out on a nature walk, where you’ll get the chance to see zebras, antelopes, and newly introduced giraffes up close. After breakfast, we’ll begin our journey back to Entebbe International Airport, with a stop for a picnic lunch and photo opportunities at the equator.
A day room at African Roots has been reserved for you to relax and freshen up.
Following a farewell dinner, your safari guide will escort you to Entebbe International Airport for your departure. Please arrive at the airport at least 2 hours before your flight. The departure lounge offers a small restaurant, duty-free shops, and comfortable seating areas.
Transfer to the airport (HB)

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 10-Day Best of Uganda Tour
touren.desc: Experience the best of Rwanda on this 10-day tour featuring thrilling gorilla trekking, captivating chimpanzee and golden monkey encounters, scenic boat rides on Lake Kivu, and immersive cultural experiences. Book your Rwanda adventure today!
highlights: Gorilla trekking in Bwindi Impenetrable Forest – spend an unforgettable hour observing habituated gorillas in their natural habitat.
Chimpanzee tracking in Kibale Forest – see chimpanzees and other primates up close on a guided forest trek.
Murchison Falls National Park adventures – game drives and a Nile boat cruise to the base of the spectacular falls.
Kazinga Channel boat cruise – spot hippos, crocodiles, elephants, and abundant birdlife in Queen Elizabeth National Park.
Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary – guided walking safari to track Uganda’s wild rhinos.
Cultural & community experiences – visits to local villages, Batwa forest communities, and hands-on cultural activities.
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Entebbe
Upon arrival at Entebbe International Airport, you will be met by your professional safari guide and transferred to your hotel. Depending on your arrival time, enjoy a relaxed evening at leisure, recovering from your journey and preparing for the adventure ahead.
Overnight: Boma Guest House
Meals: Lunch & Dinner is optional (depending on arrival time)

Day 2: Entebbe – Murchison Falls National Park
After an early breakfast, depart Entebbe and drive north-west towards Murchison Falls National Park. En route, stop at Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary for a guided walking safari, where you will track Uganda’s only wild rhinos on foot. Continue to Murchison Falls, entering the park and proceeding to your lodge.
Overnight: Twiga Safari Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 3: Murchison Falls National Park
Today is dedicated to exploring Murchison Falls National Park. Enjoy a morning game drive on the northern bank of the Nile, searching for elephants, giraffes, lions, buffaloes, and antelopes. In the afternoon, take a boat cruise along the Nile to the base of the spectacular Murchison Falls, with excellent wildlife and birdlife viewing along the riverbanks.
Overnight: Twiga Safari Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 4: Murchison Falls – Kibale Forest National Park
After breakfast, depart Murchison Falls and drive south towards Kibale Forest National Park. The journey takes you through scenic countryside, tea plantations, and rural communities. Arrive in the afternoon and relax at the lodge, surrounded by lush rainforest.
Overnight: Turaco Treetops Lodge- standard rooms
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 5: Chimpanzee Tracking & Bigodi Wetland
This morning, head to Kibale Forest for a guided chimpanzee tracking experience, offering the opportunity to observe chimpanzees and other primates in their natural habitat. In the afternoon, visit Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary for a community-led nature walk rich in birdlife and cultural encounters.
Overnight: Turaco Treetops Lodge- standard rooms
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 6: Kibale – Queen Elizabeth National Park
After breakfast, drive south to Queen Elizabeth National Park. Upon arrival, enjoy an afternoon boat cruise along the Kazinga Channel, famous for its high concentration of hippos, crocodiles, elephants, and water birds.
Overnight: Elephant Plains Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 7: Queen Elizabeth National Park
Start the day with a morning game drive, a prime area for spotting lions, buffaloes, elephants, and Uganda kob. The rest of the day is at leisure, with time to relax at the lodge and enjoy panoramic views over the park.
Overnight: Elephant Plains Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 8: Queen Elizabeth – Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
After breakfast, depart Queen Elizabeth National Park and travel south-west to Bwindi Impenetrable Forest National Park. Enjoy the scenic drive through rolling hills, terraced farmland, and forested landscapes before arriving at your lodge near the gorilla trekking sector.
Overnight: Buhoma Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 9: Gorilla Trekking in Bwindi
Today is the highlight of the safari. After an early breakfast, transfer to the park headquarters for a briefing before embarking on a guided gorilla trekking experience. Spend a magical hour observing a habituated gorilla family in the forest. Return to the lodge for relaxation and reflection on this unforgettable encounter.
Overnight: Buhoma Lodge
Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner

Day 10: Bwindi – Entebbe (Departure) – Evening Departure flight through Entebbe International Airport or Kigali International Airport recommended
After breakfast, Your Tour Guide Driver will drive you to Entebbe International Airport. (scheduled flight can be arranged at an additional fee). Transfer to Entebbe International Airport for your onward flight, marking the end of your unforgettable Ugandan safari.
Meals: Breakfast

Sabine H

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 12-Days Pearls of Uganda Safari
touren.desc: Embark on a 12-day safari through Uganda's top wildlife destinations that includes gorilla tracking in Bwindi, game drives in Murchison Falls, and a Kazinga Channel safari by boat, and much more! Book your adventure today!
highlights: Explore Murchison Falls National Park, including a thrilling boat trip to the Nile Delta and a visit to the top of the falls
Track mountain gorillas in Bwindi Impenetrable Forest, a UNESCO World Heritage site
Discover the diverse wildlife of Queen Elizabeth National Park, with a focus on the famous tree-climbing lions and a boat safari on the Kazinga Channel
Experience chimpanzee tracking and birdwatching in Kibale Forest National Park
Visit the scenic Lake Mburo National Park, featuring a night safari and sightings of Burchell’s zebras and giraffes
Journey to the Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary and encounter White Rhinos

touren.name: 10-Day Tanzania Zanzibar Holiday & Uganda Gorilla Trek
touren.desc: Set off on a thrilling gorilla trek in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park and unwind on the stunning beaches of Zanzibar. This Africa holiday package combines luxurious accommodations, cultural experiences, and diverse wildlife encounters. Unforgettable experiences await - Book now!
highlights: Encounter the rare and majestic mountain gorillas up close in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, one of the few places where these gentle giants can be observed in the wild
Relax at the Breezes Beach Club and Spa on Zanzibar, enjoying its pristine white sands, clear waters, and top-tier amenities for a perfect beach getaway
Discover the vibrant culture of Zanzibar with a spice tour, traditional dhow sailing, and visits to local markets and historic Stone Town
Experience diverse wildlife adventures, from observing gorillas in their natural habitat to spotting marine life while diving or snorkelling in the Indian Ocean
Engage with local communities, from witnessing traditional Batwa dances in Bwindi to exploring the rich cultural heritage of Zanzibar
itinerary:
Day 1: Welcome to the Former Capital of Uganda (Entebbe)
Upon your arrival at Entebbe International Airport, your guide will greet you and escort you to your guesthouse. Entebbe, a small town nestled on the shores of Lake Victoria, has a population of just 70,000. It boasts a beautiful botanical garden and offers a relaxing start to your adventure. Settle in and enjoy a refreshing traditional fruity cocktail to unwind after your journey.
Overnight at 2 Friends Beach Hotel or Karibu Guesthouse

Day 2: The Animal Kingdom of the Jungle (Entebbe, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Today, we depart for Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Known for its rich biological and botanical diversity, the park covers 331 square kilometres and is home to around 400 wild mountain gorillas—almost half of the global population of these gentle giants. Bwindi is also renowned for bird watching, internationally recognised by the African Bird Club. After a scenic drive, spend your afternoon exploring Buhoma village, visiting local handicraft markets, or learning about the Batwa tribe, an ancient people who live in the forest.
Overnight at The Haven, a community lodge in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Day 3: In Touch with the Gentle Apes (Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Begin your day with an early start and a hearty breakfast to prepare for gorilla tracking. After registration at the Uganda Wildlife Authority Ranger Station and a briefing, your adventure begins. The rangers will guide you through village gardens and into the dense, jungle-like forest, using machetes to clear the undergrowth as they lead the way to the gorillas, who are under 24-hour observation for their safety.
During your time with the gorillas, your guide will interpret their behaviours, offering insights into family dynamics. You’ll have the chance to observe mothers with their young, playful juveniles, and the dominant silverback. This hour-long experience with the gorillas is unforgettable, as they often display curiosity towards human visitors.
After your trek, return to the village for some well-deserved rest. In the afternoon, you can experience the Batwa tribe’s cultural performances, including their traditional dances and forest survival skills.
Overnight at The Haven
Day 4 – Relax at the Beaches of the Indian Ocean (Entebbe, Zanzibar)
After breakfast, transfer to Entebbe for your flight to Zanzibar via Dar es Salaam. Upon arrival at Zanzibar Airport, you’ll be transported to your beach resort. The island’s white sandy beaches and crystal-clear waters of the Indian Ocean create the perfect atmosphere for relaxation. A refreshing ocean breeze and a range of water sports and excursions await you at your resort.
Dinner and overnight at Breezes Beach Club and Spa
Days 5 – 9 – Relax and Explore Zanzibar (Zanzibar)
Spend the next few days soaking in the beauty of Zanzibar, a semi-autonomous part of Tanzania with a population of around 1 million. You have the freedom to choose from a wide array of activities that cater to all interests. Stroll through the hidden alleys of Stone Town for a taste of local culture or set sail on a traditional dhow for a sunset cruise. Indulge in a culinary adventure with a spice tour or sample freshly grilled seafood at Forodhani market.
For beach lovers, the surrounding islands offer exciting day trips, and if you’re lucky, you might even spot wild dolphins. For the more adventurous, options include diving in coral gardens, deep-sea fishing, or windsurfing. Zanzibar’s diverse experiences promise something for everyone.
Dinner and overnight at Breezes Beach Club and Spa
Day 10 – Goodbye (Zanzibar)
After breakfast, transfer from your resort to Zanzibar Airport, according to your departure schedule. As you bid farewell to this unforgettable adventure, you’ll carry with you lasting memories of Africa’s beauty. We hope to welcome you back to East Africa soon!
Before Booking, Please Read the Following Carefully
Prices are valid for 2024, 2025 and 2026.
The itinerary mentioned for this tour is an example which can be tailored to your wishes.
The lodges and hotels are subject to availability at the time of booking. In the event that a property is unavailable, we will be pleased to offer a suitable alternative of a similar range, quality, and service standard to ensure your comfort and satisfaction.
The Gorilla Tracking included in this safari depends on the availability of gorilla trekking permits at the time of booking. These permits are strictly limited and sold out in advance, especially during peak seasons of Christmas and school holidays. We therefore strongly recommend booking early.
The safari indicated here is essentially an offer. Changes in the course of the journey, the duration of your tour, the number of travellers, as well as the choice of accommodation are subject to availability, although we always endeavour to stick as closely as possible to this programme. For most itineraries, we can offer luxury, mid-range or budget versions of this safari upon request.
Sometimes it is not possible to adhere to the travel plan as described in this itinerary, due to weather and road conditions, authority regulations, organisational requirements, etc. Occasionally, the planned route of the tour has to change according to the situation on the ground. We must therefore explicitly refuse liability for adherence to the journey plan.

An unforgettable Journey Through Uganda – The Pearl of Africa. Would return in a heartbeat.
From the moment we landed in Uganda, it was clear that this country truly lives up to its name—The Pearl of Africa. Our adventure took us through breathtaking landscapes, from the lush rainforests of Bwindi to the sprawling savannas of Queen Elizabeth National Park, and every moment was nothing short of magical.
But what made this journey truly unforgettable was our guide, Emma. From the very first day, Emma proved to be not just a guide, but the perfect travel companion—knowledgeable, patient, and incredibly passionate about his country. His expertise in geography, wildlife, and especially birds turned every drive and hike into a fascinating lesson. Whether identifying rare bird species by their calls or explaining the delicate balance of the ecosystems we encountered, Emma made sure we saw Uganda through the eyes of a true nature enthusiast.
Beyond his knowledge, Emma’s warm personality, sense of humor, and unwavering enthusiasm made every moment enjoyable. He went above and beyond to ensure our comfort, answered every question with a smile, and made sure we left Uganda not just with incredible memories, but with a deep appreciation for the country’s natural beauty.
If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 3-Day Gorilla Trekking Rwanda
touren.desc: Explore Rwanda’s incredible wildlife and culture on this 3-day adventure. Track mountain gorillas and gain insight into local traditions with a visit to the Iby’iwacu Village for an unforgettable experience!
highlights: Encounter the majestic mountain gorillas up close in their natural habitat
Discover Kigali’s vibrant markets, the Gisozi Genocide Memorial, and the Kigali Museum
Enjoy breathtaking views of Rwanda’s iconic volcanic landscape as journey through the Virunga Mountains
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive at Kigali International Airport
On arrival at Kigali International Airport, you will be met by your driver/guide and your day starts with a complementary tour of the city.  Named in 1907 by Dr. Richard Kandt, the first colonial resident governor of Rwanda; Kigali city is surrounded by lush hills of green and is referred to as the land of a thousand hills. Get to visit interesting spots which may include; the craft market, Gisozi genocide memorial site & Kigali museum.
Depart for a 2 hours’ drive to Ruhengeri area the second largest town in Rwanda, with its stunning backdrop of five volcanic mountains – The Virungas. Arrive at Red rocks Intercultural Exchange centre for lunch then join the local brew makers to understand the chemistry about the process of brewing a local beer from bananas fruits. If you find the activity interesting enough you can join the women in squeezing the juice out of the bananas and drink a glass of juice (Optional). Understand how long juice takes to turn into alcohol after adding in sorghum floor. Enjoy the big glass of our local brew. Among other activities they also do basket weaving and with your visit this local community will be proud of this activity and families supported financially. Later drive to your hotel for dinner and overnight.
If you arrive early, additional options include a visit to Musanze Caves, a village walk or even a visit to the grave of famous primatologist Dian Fossey of “Gorillas in the Mist” fame who studied the gorillas of this area.
Overnight at La Palme (FB)

Day 2: Gorilla trekking in Volcanoes National Park
After an early breakfast, you will proceed to the National Park headquarters – Kinigi to meet your guides for the day. After completing our park formalities, we embark on a once in a lifetime experience of tracking mountain gorillas. Once you spot them you are allowed to spend an hour watching your closest long-lost cousin. This is perhaps one of the most breath-taking wildlife experiences on earth!!! You get so close to these awesome giant primates. You stare at each other blankly not knowing who to break the long silence first. Tracking the gorillas through the light mountain forest on the slopes of the Virunga is a magical experience. These massive primates are extremely rare, with less than 700 living today. You will get within meters of the family and be privileged to spend an hour amongst them. The trekking may be fairly strenuous; up to six hours or longer at a relatively high altitude. We encourage for your own enjoyment to make sure that you are perfectly fit and healthy.
It is important to bring good hiking boots, long trousers, gloves for protection against stinging nettles, some snacks & sweets (for you, not the gorillas) as well as sufficient water as the rain forest gets hot and humid during the day. You will spend one hour with the gorillas, but it is an hour you will never forget. Return to hotel for lunch, or Afternoon Optional Cultural Activities and later overnight.
Overnight at La Palme (FB)
DAY 3: City Tour + Goodbye
After breakfast you will bid your host goodbye and depart for Kigali. Take a complementary city tour to visit interesting spots which may include; the craft market, Gisozi genocide memorial site & Kigali museum. Also engage in last minute shopping in one of the friendliest African capitals, before returning to the airport for your onward international flight back home.
End of service

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!
