touren.name: 5-Day Gorilla Trekking Rwanda
touren.desc: Embark on a thrilling adventure from Kigali to Bwindi Impenetrable National Park. Track gorillas, explore Rwanda’s vibrant capital, and experience rich cultural encounters. Book now!
highlights: Traverse stunning landscapes as you travel from Kigali to the lush jungles of Bwindi
Experience the thrill of observing mountain gorillas in their natural habitat, guided by expert rangers
Engage with the indigenous Batwa people, known as the “keepers of the forest”, and enjoy their traditional dance performance
Explore Rwanda’s capital with visits to the craft market, Gisozi Genocide Memorial, and Kigali Museum.
Relax in comfortable accommodations, including the cosy Chez Lando Hotel in Kigali and the serene Ichumbi Lodge in Bwindi
itinerary:
Day 1: Welcome to Kigali, the capital city of Rwanda
Depending on the time of your arrival in Kigali, you will either be met by your guide or greeted by an employee of your first accommodation and taken to the hotel. Boutique Heaven Lodge is approximately 15 minutes from Kigali Airport and close to the city centre. Each room has a private terrace from where you have a wonderful view of the spacious garden. The rooms themselves are simple but comfortable and decorated in brown tones. There are two restaurants connected to your hotel.
The route covers about 10 kilometres. The journey takes about 15 – 30 minutes.
Overnight Boutique Heaven Lodge.

Day 2: The Kingdom of the African Jungle (Kigali, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Today we are heading to the Bwindi Impenetrable National Park. This park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the richest biological and botanical regions in the world. The forest covers a total area of 331 square kilometres at the southwestern end of the western Rift Valley. Bwindi’s most well-known species are the approximately 400 wild mountain gorillas that make up half of the world’s population of these gentle apes. Also highly recommended is the bird watching in Bwindi, organised by the African Bird Club. After a few hours in the car, we can take an afternoon walk in the village of Buhoma and enjoy the chance to visit one of the many handicraft markets. Alternatively, you can visit the Batwa, a native tribe in the middle of the forest.
Overnight at Ichumbi Lodge in Rushaga (Half board).

Day 3: Get in touch with the Kings of the Jungle (Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, Uganda)
Get up early for your gorilla tracking and have a well-balanced breakfast in preparation for the day. After registration at the Uganda Wildlife Authority Ranger Station and a short briefing, your gorilla tracking will start. The rangers will lead you through village gardens to a jungle-like forest. They will lead you through dense bush where the guides cut off the undergrowth with their machete and try to locate the gorillas. The mountain gorillas are under 24-hour observation for their own safety, which makes finding them much easier. Rangers use radio communication to track each gorilla family, while your guide interprets their behaviour. You’ll have the chance to observe females with their young, playful juveniles, and even the dominant silverback. Watching these magnificent creatures feed and play is an unforgettable experience. After your thrilling gorilla encounter, return to the village for the afternoon, where you can enjoy a traditional dance performance by the Batwa, the indigenous people of the forest.
Overnight at Ichumbi Lodge (Full board).
Day 4 – Drive back to Kigali, Rwanda
After breakfast, prepare for your return journey to Kigali where you’ll spend another night before flying back home.
Overnight at Boutique Heaven Lodge (Half board).

Day 5: Departure
After breakfast, start your day with a complimentary city tour. Explore fascinating spots such as the craft market, the Gisozi Genocide Memorial, and the Kigali Museum. Afterwards, your guide will escort you to the airport for your flight home. Remember to arrive 2 hours before your flight for check-in.
(BB- breakfast).

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 5-Day Rwanda Gorilla & Golden Monkey Trek
touren.desc: Explore Rwanda’s incredible wildlife and culture on this 5-day adventure. Track mountain gorillas, encounter golden monkeys, and gain insight into local traditions with a visit to the Iby’iwacu Village for an unforgettable experience!
highlights: Encounter the majestic mountain gorillas up close in their natural habitat
Explore the lush bamboo forests and observe playful golden monkeys
Discover Kigali’s vibrant markets, the Gisozi Genocide Memorial, and the Kigali Museum
Gain insight into local traditions with a visit to Iby’iwacu Village and support community conservation efforts
Enjoy breathtaking views of Rwanda’s iconic volcanic landscape as journey through the Virunga Mountains
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive at Kigali International Airport
On arrival at Kigali International Airport, you will be met by your driver/guide and be transferred to your hotel in Kigali.
Overnight at Kigali Serena (B&B).

Day 2: Kigali City Tour and travel to Ruhengeri
After breakfast, begin your day with a complimentary city tour, exploring highlights like the vibrant craft market, the moving Gisozi Genocide Memorial Site, and the Kigali Museum. Then, set off for a scenic 2-hour drive to Ruhengeri, Rwanda’s second-largest town, framed by the stunning Virunga Mountains.
Upon arrival at the Red Rocks Intercultural Exchange Centre, enjoy lunch and dive into the local brewing tradition. Learn about the fascinating process of making banana beer from local brew makers. If you’re interested, you can join the women in extracting juice from bananas and sample a glass of fresh juice (optional). Discover how this juice transforms into alcohol with the addition of sorghum flour and enjoy a taste of the local brew.
The centre also features basket weaving, which supports the local community. Your visit helps support this craft and the families involved. End your day with a drive back to your hotel for dinner and overnight stay.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 3: Golden Monkeys and visit of IBY’IWACU Village
Today is dedicated to an exciting Golden Monkey tracking adventure in Volcanoes National Park. Venture into lush bamboo forests and open glades as you head deep into golden monkey territory. Expert guides will lead you to the habitat of ‘habituated’ golden monkeys, those that have become accustomed to human presence. While viewing is limited to one hour, you’ll have the opportunity to see these charming monkeys up close in their natural environment. Watch as the adults play, feed on bamboo leaves, and leap gracefully from branch to branch, while females tenderly carry their young.
After this memorable experience, visit the Iby’iwacu Cultural Village. This non-profit organisation works to enhance the lives of reformed poachers and local communities around Volcanoes National Park by offering conservation incentives, supporting community enterprises, and facilitating livelihood projects.
Return to your hotel to refresh and enjoy lunch. Later, savour a delightful dinner and relax for the night.
Driving distance: 45 minutes.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 4: Meet the Gorillas
After an early breakfast, head to the National Park headquarters at Kinigi to meet your guides for the day. Following the completion of park formalities, embark on a once-in-a-lifetime adventure tracking mountain gorillas. Once you spot them, you’ll have the incredible opportunity to spend an hour observing these majestic primates up close. This is truly one of the most breathtaking wildlife encounters on earth! You’ll find yourself face-to-face with these magnificent giants, sharing a silent moment of mutual curiosity.
Tracking the gorillas through the lush, light mountain forest on the slopes of the Virungas is a magical experience. With fewer than 700 of these massive primates left in the wild, you’ll be privileged to get within metres of the family and spend an unforgettable hour among them. Be prepared for a potentially strenuous trek, which can last up to six hours or more at high altitude. To ensure your enjoyment, we recommend being in good physical condition. (We’ve had guests as old as 70 successfully do gorilla tracking; just let your driver guide know if you need a shorter trek.)
Remember to bring sturdy hiking boots, long trousers, gloves to protect against stinging nettles, snacks (for yourself, not the gorillas), and plenty of water, as the rainforest can be hot and humid. After your gorilla encounter, return to your hotel for lunch, followed by dinner and an overnight stay.
Driving distance: 15 – 45 minutes to the park.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 5: Depart
After breakfast your guide will be waiting to pick you up for the two-hour transfer drive to Kigali International airport for your flight home.
Before Booking, Please Read the Following Carefully
Prices are valid for 2026 and 2027.
The itinerary mentioned for this tour is an example which can be tailored to your wishes.
The lodges and hotels are subject to availability at the time of booking. In the event that a property is unavailable, we will be pleased to offer a suitable alternative of a similar range, quality, and service standard to ensure your comfort and satisfaction.
The Gorilla Tracking included in this safari depends on the availability of gorilla trekking permits at the time of booking. These permits are strictly limited and sold out in advance, especially during peak seasons of Christmas and school holidays. We therefore strongly recommend booking early.
The safari indicated here is essentially an offer. Changes in the course of the journey, the duration of your tour, the number of travellers, as well as the choice of accommodation are subject to availability, although we always endeavour to stick as closely as possible to this programme. For most itineraries, we can offer luxury, mid-range or budget versions of this safari upon request.
Sometimes it is not possible to adhere to the travel plan as described in this itinerary, due to weather and road conditions, authority regulations, organisational requirements, etc. Occasionally, the planned route of the tour has to change according to the situation on the ground. We must therefore explicitly refuse liability for adherence to the journey plan.

touren.name: 6-Day Thousand Hills of Rwanda LUXURY
touren.desc: Experience Rwanda on this exclusive 6-day tour that includes gorilla tracking, elegant accommodations, authentic African cuisine, and stunning landscapes. Book your adventure now!
highlights: Experience rare and intimate moments with Rwanda’s iconic mountain gorillas and golden monkeys in their natural habitat
Enjoy premium stays at some of Rwanda’s finest lodges and hotels, each offering unparalleled comfort and stunning views
Connect with local communities through exclusive cultural experiences, from traditional dance performances to hands-on craft workshops
Traverse Rwanda’s breathtaking landscapes, from the serene shores of Lake Kivu to the rolling hills and majestic mountains that define the region
Savour exquisite local and international cuisine, carefully crafted to provide a taste of Rwandan flavours with a touch of luxury
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive Kigali International Airport
On arrival at Kigali International Airport, you will be met by your driver/guide and be transferred to your hotel in Kigali.
Overnight at Kigali Serena Hotel (BB).

Day 2: Visit Ntarama & Nyamata Memorial + Depart for Ruhengeri
After breakfast, you’ll start your day with a visit to the Ntarama Church, a site of tragic significance during the 1994 genocide where over 5,000 people lost their lives. Located about 30 kilometers south of Kigali in the Bugasera region, the Ntarama Genocide Memorial stands as a poignant reminder of the devastating violence that occurred here. During your visit, you’ll have the opportunity to learn about the Unity and Reconciliation Commission, which plays a crucial role in Rwanda’s healing process.
Next, you’ll proceed to the Nyamata Memorial Site, another church where approximately 2,500 people were killed. This site has become a powerful symbol of the horrific treatment of women during the genocide. At Nyamata, you’ll encounter graphic and audiovisual displays that focus on the mass rape, brutalization of women, and the deliberate use of HIV as a weapon of genocide.
After returning to Kigali for lunch, you’ll set off on a scenic two-hour drive to Ruhengeri, Rwanda’s second-largest town. As you approach, you’ll be greeted by the breathtaking backdrop of the Virunga Mountains, a chain of five majestic volcanoes. Upon arrival, you’ll settle into your accommodation and prepare for dinner and a comfortable overnight stay at your hotel.
Driving distance: 2 hours.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 3: Gorilla Tracking
Start your day with an early breakfast before heading to the National Park headquarters in Kinigi, where you’ll meet your guides for the day. After completing park formalities, you’ll embark on a once-in-a-lifetime experience: tracking mountain gorillas.
When you finally spot these magnificent creatures, you’ll have the unique opportunity to spend an hour observing them up close. This is one of the most breathtaking wildlife encounters on earth. You’ll be within metres of these gentle giants, staring at each other in awe. The experience of trekking through the light mountain forest on the slopes of the Virunga to find them is nothing short of magical. With fewer than 700 mountain gorillas remaining in the world, this encounter is truly a rare privilege.
The trek can be challenging, lasting up to six hours or more at a relatively high altitude. To ensure you enjoy the experience fully, it’s important to be fit and healthy. If you’re concerned about the trek’s difficulty, ask your driver-guide to request a shorter route for you (People as old as 70 have successfully completed gorilla tracking, so don’t worry!).
Be sure to bring good hiking boots, long trousers, gloves for protection against stinging nettles, snacks and sweets (for yourself, not the gorillas), and plenty of water, as the rainforest can get hot and humid during the day.
After your unforgettable hour with the gorillas, you’ll return to your hotel for lunch, followed by dinner and an overnight stay.
Driving distance: 15 minutes.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 4: Golden Monkey tracking and visit of Iby’iwacu cultural village
Today, you’ll embark on an exciting adventure: tracking golden monkeys in Volcanoes National Park. As you enter the dense bamboo forests, interspersed with open glades, you’ll be guided deep into golden monkey territory. Your guides will lead you to groups of habituated golden monkeys—those accustomed to human presence. Though the viewing time is limited to one hour, you’ll have the opportunity to observe these lively creatures up close in their natural habitat. Watch as adult monkeys frolic in plain view, feeding on bamboo leaves and leaping from perch to perch, while females carry their young, completely at ease among onlookers.
In the afternoon, you have several optional activities to choose from. You can visit the Iby’iwacu cultural village to experience a traditional dance, meet a local medicine man, or even explore the house of a local king. These cultural activities are popular with visitors and provide alternative incomes to reformed poachers. Another option is to visit local brewers and learn about the process of brewing beer from bananas. For some extra fun, you can join the women in squeezing juice from the bananas and enjoy a glass of the fresh juice. Additionally, you can discover the art of basket weaving, an important cultural practice in Rwanda. Your participation in these activities helps support the local community financially.
Driving distance: 15 minutes to the park.
Overnight at Virunga Lodge (FB).

Day 5: Visit Gisenyi
After breakfast, you’ll set off on an intriguing trip to visit local brew makers, where you’ll learn about the chemistry involved in brewing beer from bananas. If you’re interested, you can join the women in squeezing juice from the bananas and enjoy a glass of the fresh juice (optional). You’ll also discover how the juice is transformed into alcohol after adding sorghum flour and get a chance to savor a big glass of the local brew. The community also practices basket weaving, and your visit will provide financial support to these local families.
Later, you’ll make your way to Gisenyi, arriving in time for lunch and checking in at your hotel. The afternoon will be spent exploring Gisenyi, the largest port on the Rwandan shore of Lake Kivu, accompanied by your guide. You can take a short boat ride to one of the islands in Lake Kivu or simply stroll through the town, enjoying the lively local market or wandering the leafy streets while admiring the old colonial buildings.
Dinner and overnight Lake Kivu Serena.

Day 6: Departure
After breakfast, depending on your flight departure time, you’ll spend the early part of the day on the Rwandan shore of Lake Kivu. Take a refreshing swim in this stunning lake, which covers an area of 2,700 km² (1,040 sq. mi) and sits at an altitude of 1,460 m (4,790 ft) above sea level. The lake lies in a rift valley, causing volcanic activity and giving it a remarkable depth of up to 480 m (1,575 ft), making it the fifteenth deepest lake in the world. Surrounded by majestic mountains, Lake Kivu is one of the African Great Lakes and straddles the border between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda.
Later, you’ll depart on a three-hour drive to Kigali, where you’ll continue to Kigali International Airport for your flight home.
A dayroom at Kigali Serena can be provided upon request.
Driving distance: 3 hours.

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 7-Day Best of Rwanda Mid-Range Tour
touren.desc: Explore Rwanda on a thrilling 7-day adventure, featuring gorilla encounters, chimpanzee tracking, serene Lake Kivu experiences, and cultural visits to Imbabazi and scenic twin lakes.
highlights: Encounter the majestic mountain gorillas in their natural habitat in the Virunga Mountains with expert guidance
Track and observe endangered chimpanzees in one of Africa’s largest montane forests, the Nyungwe National Park
Enjoy the serene beauty of Lake Kivu with opportunities for swimming, boat rides, and exploring Gisenyi
Discover the legacy of Rosamond Halsey Carr at the Imbabazi Orphanage and enjoy traditional entertainment at this historic site
Admire the stunning twin lakes of Bulera and Ruhondo surrounded by dramatic hills and waterfalls
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrive Kigali International Airport
On arrival at Kigali International Airport, you will be met by your driver/guide and be
transferred to your hotel in Kigali.
Driving distance: 15 minutes.
Overnight at Heaven Boutique Hotel (B&B).

Day 2: Depart for Nyungwe National Park
Start your day with a hearty breakfast before setting off on a scenic 6 ½ hour drive to Nyungwe National Park. Along the way, you’ll have the chance to explore some of Rwanda’s most significant cultural sites.
Your first stop is the National Museum at Butare, an insightful institution donated by the Belgian government in 1989. The museum was developed in collaboration with the Royal Museum for Central Africa in Belgium and offers a fascinating look into Rwanda’s cultural heritage. Exhibits showcase ethnographic collections, traditional ceramics, and basketry, which are among the finest handicrafts of the region. Remarkably, the museum remained untouched during the 1994 civil war.
After a leisurely lunch in Butare, continue your journey to Nyungwe National Park. You’ll arrive in time for dinner and a relaxing overnight stay at your hotel.
Driving distance: 6 ½ hours.
Overnight at Nyungwe Top View Hotel (FB)

Day 3: Chimpanzee Trekking and Nature Walk
Begin your day with an early breakfast before setting off for an exciting chimpanzee trek in Nyungwe National Park. Your hotel will provide a packed lunch for the journey. Nyungwe, the largest block of montane forest in East and Central Africa, is a biodiversity hotspot. The park is home to a variety of species, including the endangered chimpanzee population of around 400 individuals. While the thrill of spotting these incredible creatures is undeniable, it’s important to note that sightings aren’t guaranteed due to their unpredictable movements. However, the rangers will do everything possible to locate them, keeping in constant radio contact to monitor their whereabouts.
After your trek, return to the lodge for a well-deserved lunch. In the afternoon, embark on a nature walk through the lush forest, where you’ll visit the picturesque Kamiranzovu Waterfall. Nyungwe’s forest is a paradise for nature lovers, boasting over 200 tree species, more than 280 bird species, and over 100 types of colourful butterflies, making it a true feast for the senses. Conclude your day with dinner and an overnight stay at Nyungwe Top View Lodge.
Driving distance: approximately 50 km.
Overnight at Nyungwe Top View Hotel (FB).

Day 4: Depart for Gisenyi and Lake Kivu
After breakfast, continue your journey to Gisenyi, Rwanda’s largest port on the shores of Lake Kivu. You’ll arrive in time to check in and enjoy lunch at your hotel.
In the afternoon, you have the option to swim in Lake Kivu, one of the African Great Lakes, which straddles the border between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda. Spend the day exploring this vibrant port city. Your guide will be delighted to share insights as you take in the sights. You can also enjoy a boat ride to one of the islands in Lake Kivu (included) or explore the town at your leisure, soaking up the local atmosphere at the market or wandering through the leafy streets to admire the old colonial buildings. End the day with dinner and an overnight stay at Kivu Lodge.
Driving distance: 3 hours.
Overnight at Kivu Lodge (FB).

Day 5: Depart for Ruhengeri
Your day begins with a departure to Ruhengeri, with a memorable stop at the Imbabazi Orphanage. Founded in 1994 by Rosamond Halsey Carr (1912-2006), the orphanage has been a sanctuary for over 400 children, although it has since closed. Roz’s remarkable life and love for Rwanda are beautifully captured in her book Land of a Thousand Hills: My Life in Rwanda, co-authored with her niece, Ann Howard Halsey. Roz was also honoured with the Volvo for Life Award in 2004, and a film about her life is currently in production. During your visit, you can stroll through the homestead, admiring the flowers she planted, and enjoy a cup of tea or coffee while watching local dancers perform traditional entertainment.
Afterward, continue your journey to the Ruhengeri area, Rwanda’s second-largest town, set against the breathtaking backdrop of the Virunga volcanic mountains. You’ll arrive in time for check-in and lunch.
In the afternoon, drive to the twin lakes of Bulera and Ruhondo, located at the base of the Muhabura volcano near the Ugandan border. These lakes are surrounded by steep hills and framed by the majestic Virunga mountains. The deep blue waters, steep hills, and tall waterfalls create a stunning scene, offering fantastic photo opportunities at the viewpoint.
Driving distance: 15 minutes.
Overnight at Mountain Gorilla View Lodge (FB).

Day 6: Gorilla Tracking
You will be up early on the morning of your Gorilla tracking for a good breakfast in preparation for the day’s physical activity. You’ll report for registration at the ranger station by 8 AM. After a briefing by the rangers, your gorilla trek will start. The trek usually takes between three and six hours so a reasonable degree of fitness is required, as well as adequate walking boots or shoes.
The rangers will guide you through the fields, up the gentle slopes of the volcanoes and into the bamboo forest. The mountain gorillas are under 24 hour monitoring for their own safety, so you’re almost guaranteed to see them. The rangers monitor each gorilla family by keeping in close radio contact.
In part, due to gorilla tourism, the population of gorillas is slowly increasing but the species remains critically endangered. Gorillas and humans share almost identical genetic material and their health must not be compromised by coming into contact with human diseases (via our food, for example). To reduce transmission of disease from humans to gorillas, you will also be advised to keep a distance of 7 metres from the gorillas.
Once you have located the gorilla family, you will be asked to leave all your belongings to one side, and approach the gentle giants with just your camera. The guides will interpret the animals’ behaviour for you. Females and their young, young males and even a dominant Silverback may be seen feeding, playing, interacting with each other, or just sleeping! You will never forget your encounter with the gorillas. They will be quite curious to see you! You are allowed exactly one hour with the gorillas before returning to base.
Tracking the mountain gorillas is a physically demanding experience. In the afternoon, you may want to rest, or you may wish to go on a village walk, visit the local market or visit the grave of famous primatologist Dian Fossey of “Gorillas in the Mist” fame who studied the gorillas of this area.
In the afternoon, optional activities may be a visit to the Iby’iwacu cultural village to watch a cultural dance, meet a local medicine man, or even discover the house of a local king. These cultural activities are popular with visitors and offer alternative incomes to reformed poachers. Another option is to take a trip to the local brewers to understand the chemical process behind brewing local beer from bananas. Just for fun, you can even join the women in squeezing the juice out of the bananas and get a taste for yourself. Other activities you may learn about are basket weaving, an important cultural activity in Rwanda. Through your visits and the activities you participate in, you’ll help support the local community financially.
Driving distance: 15 minutes.
Overnight at Mountain Gorilla View Lodge (FB).

Day 7: City Tour and Shopping + Depart
After breakfast, your driver will collect you for the two-hour drive to Kigali. Depending on the time of your flight, you may have a chance for a complementary city tour of one of Africa’s most well-run and friendly capital cities. Interesting optional spots to visit may include the craft market, Gisozi genocide memorial site and Kigali museum. You will have a chance to do some last-minute souvenir shopping here or at the airport before your flight back home.
A dayroom at Kigali Serena can be provided upon request.
Driving distance: 2 Hours.
Meal Plan: BB.

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

He went above and beyond to ensure our comfort, answered every question with a smile, and made sure we left Uganda not just with incredible memories, but with a deep appreciation for the country’s natural beauty.
If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 10-Days Rwanda Primates, Wildlife & Cultural
touren.desc: Experience the best of Rwanda on this 10-day tour featuring thrilling gorilla trekking, captivating chimpanzee and golden monkey encounters, scenic boat rides on Lake Kivu, and immersive cultural experiences. Book your Rwanda adventure today!
highlights: Classic African wildlife safari in Akagera National Park, Rwanda’s only savannah park.
Scenic boat cruise on Lake Ihema to spot wildlife & also a Sunset cruise on Lake Kivu
Gorilla trek in Volcanoes & Chimpanzee trekking in Nyungwe
Exciting canopy walk high above the forest floor in Nyungwe
Discover Rwanda’s breathtaking landscapes, wildlife, and rich cultural heritage.
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Kigali
Upon arrival at Kigali International Airport, you will be warmly welcomed by your professional safari guide who will brief you about the upcoming adventure.
You will then transfer to your hotel in Kigali for relaxation before embarking on a short city tour. Highlights include a visit to the Kigali Genocide Memorial, a powerful and moving site that commemorates the victims of the 1994 genocide and offers deep insight into Rwanda’s history and resilience.
Depending on arrival time, you may also explore local craft markets or enjoy panoramic views of the city’s rolling hills.
Overnight: Kigali Serena Hotel in a Standard room
Meal Plan: Dinner

Day 2: Transfer to Akagera National Park
After breakfast, depart Kigali for Akagera National Park, located in eastern Rwanda near the Tanzanian border. The drive takes approximately 2–3 hours, offering scenic views of Rwanda’s countryside.
Upon arrival, check in at your lodge and enjoy lunch before heading out for an afternoon game drive in the park’s savannah plains.
Akagera is home to an impressive range of wildlife including lions, elephants, giraffes, zebras, buffaloes, and numerous antelope species. Bird enthusiasts will also find the park rewarding with over 500 bird species recorded.
Overnight: Akagera Safari Camp in cottage
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 3: Game Drive & Boat Trip on Lake Ihema
Start the day with an early morning game drive, the best time to spot predators and other wildlife as they begin their day in the cool morning hours.
Explore the northern sector of Akagera National Park, where open savannah landscapes increase the chances of seeing large mammals including lions, elephants, and buffalo.
In the afternoon, enjoy an afternoon Scheduled boat cruise on Lake Ihema, the park’s largest lake. The cruise offers excellent opportunities to see hippos, Nile crocodiles, and a variety of water birds such as African fish eagles, herons, and kingfishers.
Overnight: Akagera Safari Camp in cottage
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 4: Transfer to Nyungwe Forest National Park
After breakfast, depart for Nyungwe Forest National Park in southwestern Rwanda.
This scenic drive showcases Rwanda’s beautiful countryside, passing through rolling hills, traditional villages, and vast tea plantations.
Arrive in the afternoon and check in at your lodge located near the forest. Spend the rest of the day relaxing while enjoying views of the lush rainforest canopy.
Overnight: Munazi Lodge – Nyungwe
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 5: Chimpanzee Tracking & Canopy Walk
Wake up early for an exciting chimpanzee trekking experience in Nyungwe Forest National Park.
Accompanied by experienced trackers, you will hike through the dense rainforest in search of chimpanzees. Once located, you will spend one hour observing them, watching their social interactions, feeding habits, and playful behavior.
In the afternoon, experience the famous Zipline & Nyungwe Canopy Walk, a suspended bridge high above the forest floor that offers breathtaking views of the rainforest and surrounding mountains.
Overnight: Munazi Lodge – Nyungwe
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 6: Transfer to Lake Kivu – Sunset Boat Cruise
After breakfast, depart Nyungwe Forest National Park and begin your scenic drive toward the beautiful shores of Lake Kivu, one of Africa’s Great Lakes. The journey offers breathtaking views of Rwanda’s rolling hills, lush countryside, and charming lakeside towns.
Arrive in the vibrant lakeside town of Gisenyi, located on the northern shores of the lake, and check in at the elegant Kivu Serena Hotel. Nestled along the tranquil shoreline, the hotel offers spectacular lake views, beautiful gardens, and a relaxing atmosphere perfect for unwinding after the forest adventures in Nyungwe.
In the late afternoon, head out for a sunset boat cruise on Lake Kivu. As the boat gently glides across the calm waters, enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding hills and small islands while the golden sunset reflects beautifully across the lake. This relaxing experience offers excellent opportunities for photography and a chance to appreciate the serene beauty of one of Rwanda’s most scenic destinations.
Return to Kivu Serena Hotel for dinner and a peaceful overnight stay by the lake.
Overnight: Kivu Serena Hotel
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 7: Transfer to Volcanoes National Park
After breakfast, depart for Volcanoes National Park in northern Rwanda.
The drive takes you along the scenic shores of Lake Kivu, one of Africa’s most beautiful lakes. You may stop for lunch and enjoy the spectacular views before continuing the journey.
Arrive in the evening with magnificent views of the Virunga Volcanoes that form the dramatic backdrop of the park.
Overnight: Mountain Gorilla View Lodge
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 8: Mountain Gorilla Trekking
Today is the highlight of the safari: mountain gorilla trekking in Volcanoes National Park.
After an early breakfast, transfer to the park headquarters for a briefing by park rangers. You will then begin the trek through bamboo forests and volcanic slopes in search of a habituated gorilla family.
The trek can take 2–8 hours depending on the gorillas’ location, but the reward is extraordinary. Once you encounter the gorillas, you will spend one unforgettable hour observing them in their natural habitat.
This close encounter with these gentle giants is often described as one of the most profound wildlife experiences on Earth.
Overnight: Mountain Gorilla View Lodge
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 9: Golden Monkey Tracking & Cultural Experience
After breakfast, head back into Volcanoes National Park for golden monkey tracking.
These rare and playful primates are endemic to the Virunga Mountains and are known for their energetic movements through the bamboo forest.
In the afternoon, visit the Iby’Iwacu Cultural Village, where you will experience traditional Rwandan culture. Activities include traditional dances, storytelling, local crafts, and insights into Rwanda’s cultural heritage.
Overnight: Mountain Gorilla View Lodge
Meal Plan: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

Day 10: Return to Kigali & Departure
After breakfast, drive back to Kigali.
Depending on your departure schedule, you may visit local craft markets or enjoy lunch in the city before transferring to Kigali International Airport for your outbound flight.
This marks the end of your unforgettable Rwanda safari.
Meal Plan: Breakfast

We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 5-Day Kenya Safari
touren.desc: Book your unforgettable Kenya safari, featuring the Wildebeest Migration, Big Cat sightings, and breathtaking views of Mount Kilimanjaro. Book your adventure today!
highlights: Explore one of Africa’s premier wildlife destinations, known for its rich biodiversity and the spectacular annual migration of millions of wildebeests
Enjoy breathtaking views of Africa’s highest peak from Amboseli National Park, a perfect backdrop for observing large elephant herds
Experience an intimate walking safari on Crescent Island, offering close-up views of herbivores like giraffes and zebras in a predator-free environment
Discover the beauty of Lake Naivasha with a relaxing boat safari, providing opportunities to spot hippos and diverse bird species in a serene setting
Immerse yourself in Kenya’s diverse landscapes, from the dramatic escarpments and vast savannas to lush lake shores and rugged parklands
itinerary:
Day 1: Nairobi – Masai Mara
Depart from your Nairobi city hotel after breakfast, or be picked up from the airport if arriving early in the morning, and head to Masai Mara. Enjoy a stop at the Escarpment View Point for a breathtaking view of the Great Rift Valley. Continue your journey down to the valley floor, passing through Mai Mahiu town, and make your way to the Masai town of Narok for a brief stop before reaching Masai Mara. Known as the new 8th Wonder of the World, this immense reserve is part of the expansive Serengeti plains and is renowned for its spectacular wildebeest migration and its abundance of the Big Five: lion, elephant, leopard, rhino, and Cape buffalo.
Arrive in time for lunch at the camp and enjoy a short rest. In the afternoon, set out on a game drive through the reserve, where you might witness predators hunting for their evening meal while the Masai people guide their cattle back home. Return to your lodge for dinner and a relaxing evening at leisure.
Overnight stay at Mara Leisure Camp (Full Board)

Day 2: Masai Mara Full Day
Today, we will spend the entire day in the reserve to fully experience the incredible wildebeest migration. Instead of an early morning game drive at 6:30 am, we will depart at 8:00 am after breakfast, bringing along picnic lunch boxes.
We’ll spend the mid-morning and early afternoon exploring the reserve, where you’ll have the chance to see a variety of wildlife, including the elusive Big Five.
Between July and October, the highlight is the annual wildebeest migration. Witness the herds in vast numbers on the plains, and look for the dramatic river crossings at Mara River, where crocodiles prey on the migrating animals. This event is truly a spectacle and has been recognised as one of the Wonders of the World. Be sure to bring your camera to capture this amazing sight!
In the afternoon, we will return to the Lodge for freshening up, followed by dinner and a relaxing evening at your leisure.
Overnight stay at Mara Leisure Camp (Full Board)

Day 3: Masai Mara – Lake Naivasha
After breakfast, depart from your Lodge/Camp in Masai Mara and drive past Narok town to Lake Naivasha, located on the floor of the Great Rift Valley. Check in at your Lodge/Camp on the lake’s shores and enjoy lunch.
In the afternoon, you have several exciting options:
Hells Gate National Park: Explore this park on foot or by bicycle, with opportunities for rock climbing and walking through deep gorges.
Boat Safari on Lake Naivasha: Experience birdwatching and spot hippos as the sun sets. The boat safari includes a visit to Crescent Island Private Game Sanctuary, where you can embark on a walking safari. Here, you can get close to giraffes and other herbivores, and even chase a herd of zebras. With no big cats on the island, it’s a unique opportunity to engage with wildlife up close, away from your safari vehicle.
Return to the camp/lodge in the evening followed by dinner
Overnight at Lake Naivasha Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 4: Lake Naivasha – Amboseli National Park
After an early breakfast, depart from Lake Naivasha and drive back to Nairobi before heading south to Amboseli National Park, located at the base of Africa’s highest peak, Mount Kilimanjaro. Known informally as “Kilimanjaro’s Royal Court,” Amboseli is famous for its large herds of free-ranging African elephants, along with wildebeests, giraffes, lions, monkeys, zebras, hyenas, and antelope, all set against the stunning backdrop of Mt. Kilimanjaro.
After a 4-hour drive, arrive in the early afternoon, check in at Kibo Safari Camp, and enjoy lunch and a short rest. In the afternoon, explore the Amboseli plains on a game drive, then return to the camp at dusk for dinner.
Overnight stay at Kibo Safari Camp (Full Board)

Day 5: Amboseli – Namanga (Or Nairobi)
Start your day with an early breakfast before heading out for a game drive in Amboseli. Enjoy the drive through the park in the morning and mid-morning before exiting.
Drive back to Nairobi and arrive in the early afternoon, where you will be dropped off at your city hotel or Jomo Kenyatta Airport for your flight home.
Alternatively, you can proceed to the Namanga border to connect with a safari in Arusha, Tanzania.
(Breakfast)

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. touren.name: 6-Day Best of Kenya Fly-in Safari
touren.desc: Discover Kenya’s top wildlife spots on a luxury safari. Encounter rare species in Samburu, explore Ol Pejeta’s Big Five, and witness the Masai Mara migration. Enjoy premium lodgings and rich cultural experiences. Book your unforgettable adventure today!
highlights: Fly-In Safari Convenience – Skip long drives with seamless flights between Nairobi, Samburu, and Masai Mara, while enjoying breathtaking aerial views of Kenya’s landscapes.
Luxury Accommodation – Stay in Ashnil Samburu and Ashnil Mara Camps, offering modern comfort, prime locations inside the reserves, and exceptional hospitality.
Immerse yourself in the rich cultures and traditions of the vibrant local tribes including the Maasai Communities
Unforgettable Game Drives – Twice-daily safaris in 4×4 vehicles with expert guides, giving you the chance to spot the Big Five, rare Samburu species, and the Mara’s thriving wildlife.
Exclusive Masai Mara Experience – Enjoy all-inclusive drinks, freshly prepared meals, and a special sundowner cocktail while overlooking the savannah.
itinerary:
Day 1: Nairobi – Samburu Game Reserve (Home to Special Big 5)
Transfer to Wilson Airport for your morning flight to Buffalo Springs. Upon arrival, you’ll be met and transferred to Ashnil Samburu Camp, located in the heart of the Samburu ecosystem.
Enjoy lunch at the camp, followed by an afternoon game drive. The Samburu Reserve is known for its semi-arid landscapes, rocky outcrops, and unique wildlife such as the Beisa Oryx, Reticulated Giraffe, Somali Ostrich, and Grevy’s Zebra.
Overnight at Ashnil Samburu Camp (FB)

Day 2: Samburu Game Reserve – Full Day Safari
Start the day with an early morning game drive when animals are most active. Return to the camp for breakfast and spend the mid-morning at leisure, enjoying the views and amenities at the camp.
After lunch, embark on an afternoon game drive exploring the vast Samburu plains, riverine forests, and dry riverbeds. Keep an eye out for elephants, lions, leopards, and buffaloes, as well as Samburu’s unique species.
Overnight at Ashnil Samburu Camp (FB)

Day 3: Samburu – Masai Mara National Reserve
Enjoy a final early morning game drive in Samburu before transferring to Buffalo Springs Airstrip for your flight to Ol Kiombo Airstrip in the Masai Mara.
Upon arrival, you will be transferred to Ashnil Mara Camp. Enjoy lunch at the camp before heading out for an afternoon game drive in the Masai Mara, home to the Big 5 and vast savannah landscapes.
Overnight at Ashnil Mara Camp (FB + AI)

Day 4: Masai Mara National Reserve – Full Day Safari
After an early breakfast, set out on a morning game drive. The Mara is famous for its exceptional wildlife density, and you have excellent chances of spotting lions, cheetahs, elephants, giraffes, zebras, and buffaloes.
Return to the camp for lunch and relax. In the afternoon, enjoy another game drive, exploring different parts of the reserve and spotting wildlife along the Mara River.
Overnight at Ashnil Mara Camp (FB + AI)

Day 5: Masai Mara – Full Day Safari with Sundowner Experience
Start the day with an early morning game drive in the Masai Mara. Return to the camp for breakfast, followed by leisure time or optional activities.
After lunch, embark on your afternoon game drive. In the evening, enjoy a sundowner cocktail experience in the savannah, watching the golden sun set over the Mara plains.
Overnight at Ashnil Mara Camp (FB + AI)

Day 6: Masai Mara – Nairobi Departure
Enjoy a final morning game drive if time permits, then transfer to Ol Kiombo Airstrip for your flight back to Wilson Airport, Nairobi. From there, continue your transfer to your hotel or onward departure from Nairobi.

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 7-Day Kenya Luxury Safari
touren.desc: Discover Kenya’s top wildlife spots on a luxury safari. Encounter rare species in Samburu, explore Ol Pejeta’s Big Five, and witness the Masai Mara migration. Enjoy premium lodgings and rich cultural experiences. Book your unforgettable adventure today!
highlights: Spot rare species like the Gerenuk antelope, Reticulated Giraffe, Grevy’s Zebra, and the unique Blue-Necked Ostrich in Samburu Game Reserve
Discover the Ewaso Ng’iro River, a vital water source that supports diverse wildlife and bird species in the semi-arid Samburu Reserve
Immerse yourself in the rich cultures and traditions of the vibrant local tribes including the Samburu and Maasai Communities
Witness one of the world’s greatest ornithological spectacles with abundant bird species, including flamingos and pelicans at Lake Nakuru
Stand on the imaginary line that divides the world into northern and southern hemispheres at the Equator
itinerary:
Day 1: Nairobi- Samburu Game Reserve (Home to Special Big 5)
After breakfast at your city hotel, you’ll depart Nairobi (or be picked up at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport if arriving early) and head north, passing coffee plantations and the iconic Mt. Kenya. You’ll continue towards the northern region of Kenya, home to the Samburu tribe, cousins of the Maasai. This semi-arid region, though rugged and hot under the equatorial sun, is renowned for its dramatic landscapes and unique wildlife species found only here. Look out for rare animals such as the Beisa Oryx, Reticulated Giraffe, Somali Ostrich, and Grevy’s Zebra.
Arrive in time for lunch, followed by an afternoon game drive before returning to the lodge.
Overnight at Elephant Bedroom Camp (FB)

Day 2: Samburu Game Reserve
Spend the day exploring the Samburu Reserve with morning and afternoon game drives. The Ewaso Ng’iro River that runs through the reserve is a lifeline for the rare species that inhabit the area. Though smaller than some other parks, Samburu offers an unforgettable wildlife experience. Keep an eye out for Gerenuk antelope, leopards, lions, cheetahs, and hyenas, among other fascinating creatures. Birdwatchers will also be delighted by the abundance of birdlife, including some endangered species along the river.
Overnight at Elephant Bedroom Camp (FB)

Day 3: Samburu National Reserve – SweetWaters (Ol Pejeta Conservancy)
After breakfast, depart Samburu and travel south to Ol Pejeta Conservancy, situated between the Aberdares and Mt. Kenya. This 360 sq. km wildlife sanctuary, also known as SweetWaters, is dedicated to conserving wildlife and supporting community development. It is home to the Big Five, including the endangered black and white rhino, as well as a chimpanzee sanctuary.
Arrive in time for lunch, followed by a visit to the Chimpanzee Sanctuary and a chance to see one of the tame rhinos before heading out for an evening game drive.
Overnight at Ol Pejeta House (FB)

Day 4: SweetWaters (Ol Pejeta Conservancy) – Lake Nakuru National Park
Enjoy breakfast before departing Ol Pejeta and heading to Lake Nakuru National Park, a world-renowned destination for birdwatchers. Arrive mid-morning, check into the lodge, and enjoy lunch. After a short rest, head out on an evening game drive at 4:00 PM. The park is a sanctuary for both black and white rhinos, as well as the Rothschild Giraffe. You’ll also encounter birdlife such as flamingos, African Fish Eagles, and pelicans.
Return to the lodge in the evening
Overnight at Sarova Lion Hill Lodge (FB)

Day 5: Lake Nakuru National Park – Masai Mara Game Reserve
After breakfast, depart Lake Nakuru and journey through the scenic Rift Valley to the world-famous Masai Mara Game Reserve. Passing through the town of Narok, you’ll arrive at the lodge in time for lunch. In the afternoon, enjoy a game drive in search of the Big Five before returning to the lodge at dusk.
Overnight at Mara Serena Safari Lodge (FB)

Day 6: Masai Mara Game Reserve
Spend a full day in the Masai Mara, one of the world’s most celebrated wilderness areas. Enjoy early morning and afternoon game drives, where you’ll encounter a wide variety of mammals, including Masai Giraffe, buffalo, zebra, and gazelles. You can also opt for an exciting hot air balloon ride at an additional cost.
Alternatively, you can take a full day game drive with a picnic lunch to maximise your time in this iconic reserve. Return to the lodge in the late afternoon.
Overnight at Mara Serena Safari Lodge (FB)

Day 7: Masai Mara Game Reserve – Nairobi
After breakfast, depart the Masai Mara and drive back to Nairobi, arriving in time for lunch. Afterward, transfer to the airport for your onward flight or opt for a flight to the South Coast of Kenya for a beach holiday extension.

An unforgettable Journey Through Uganda – The Pearl of Africa. Would return in a heartbeat.
From the moment we landed in Uganda, it was clear that this country truly lives up to its name—The Pearl of Africa. Our adventure took us through breathtaking landscapes, from the lush rainforests of Bwindi to the sprawling savannas of Queen Elizabeth National Park, and every moment was nothing short of magical.
But what made this journey truly unforgettable was our guide, Emma. From the very first day, Emma proved to be not just a guide, but the perfect travel companion—knowledgeable, patient, and incredibly passionate about his country. His expertise in geography, wildlife, and especially birds turned every drive and hike into a fascinating lesson. Whether identifying rare bird species by their calls or explaining the delicate balance of the ecosystems we encountered, Emma made sure we saw Uganda through the eyes of a true nature enthusiast.
Beyond his knowledge, Emma’s warm personality, sense of humor, and unwavering enthusiasm made every moment enjoyable. He went above and beyond to ensure our comfort, answered every question with a smile, and made sure we left Uganda not just with incredible memories, but with a deep appreciation for the country’s natural beauty.
If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 4-Day Kenya Beach Resort Package
touren.desc: Escape to Kenya's stunning Indian Ocean coast with this Mombasa beach holiday. Relax on Diani Beach, enjoy snorkelling and boat rides, and savour gourmet seafood. Book your dream beach getaway today!
highlights: Relax on the pristine white sands of Diani Beach and take in the breathtaking views and crystal-clear waters
Enjoy a range of activities including glass-bottom boat rides, snorkelling in vibrant coral reefs, and exploring the charming Wasini Island (inquire about more activities at the hotel’s reception)
Savour the renowned seafood platter at Sands Nomad’s restaurant, a local favourite that attracts both visitors and residents
Experience the unique Swahili culture of Kenya’s Coast, a rich mixture of heritage and traditions that offers a truly magical atmosphere
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Mombasa
Upon your arrival at Mombasa Airport, a friendly guide will welcome you and assist with your transfer to Sands Nomad. Enjoy a smooth journey to your seaside retreat.
Overnight stay at Sands Nomad.
Day 2 & 3: Discover Diani Beach at Sands Nomad
Situated on the pristine Diani Beach, Sands Nomad invites you to unwind and soak in the breathtaking views. Embrace the opportunity to partake in exciting activities such as glass-bottom boat excursions, snorkelling in vibrant coral reefs, or exploring the charming Wasini Island. For a memorable dining experience, be sure to indulge in the hotel’s celebrated seafood platter, which attracts many visitors and locals.
2 overnight stays at Sands Nomad.

Day 4: Departure
After a leisurely breakfast, your guide will transfer you back to Mombasa Airport for your return flight home.
Note: Please ensure you check in for international flights at least two hours before departure.

touren.name: 11-Days From Bush to Beach Kenya Safari
touren.desc: Embark on an 11-day bush to beach safari, combining thrilling game drives in Tsavo East with luxurious relaxation on the stunning shores of Diani Beach. Book now!
highlights: Experience thrilling game drives in Tsavo East National Park, where you’ll encounter dust-red elephants, majestic lions, rhinos, and the infamous “man-eaters” of Tsavo
Explore the historical Fort Jesus and stroll through Old Town Mombasa, immersing yourself in Swahili culture and marvelling at centuries-old architecture
Relax on the pristine shores of Diani Beach, with crystal-clear waters and ample time for swimming, sunbathing, or indulging in beachside activities
Discover the ancient Gedi Ruins hidden in a lush forest, where you’ll uncover the fascinating history of a 12th-century Swahili settlement
Enjoy luxurious stays in stunning lodges and beach resorts, offering the perfect blend of adventure and relaxation after your day’s explorations
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Mombasa/Diani Beach
Upon arrival, you will be greeted by our customer service representative, who will assist with your transfer to the beach hotel. On arrival, enjoy a refreshing non-alcoholic welcome drink and a brief orientation about your vacation.
Dinner and overnight at Jacaranda Indian Ocean Beach Resort.

Day 2: Explore Mombasa
Wake up to the soothing sounds of the Indian Ocean and enjoy a relaxed breakfast at the hotel. Afterward, embark on an excursion to Mombasa’s Old Town and the historic Fort Jesus.
Fort Jesus, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was built by the Portuguese between 1593 and 1596. Designed by Italian architect Cairati, it stands as an impressive example of 16th-century Portuguese military architecture, even though parts of it are damaged. The fort, strategically located on a coral ridge at the entrance of the harbour, has witnessed numerous battles for control. Over the years, it has served as soldier barracks, a prison, and now houses a museum showcasing an extensive collection of ceramics and pottery, reflecting the diverse cultures that once traded along the coast.
You’ll also explore the Old Town, renowned for its architectural gems like filigree timber balconies, carved wooden doors, and colonial and Indian designs. Old Town is home to more than 20 mosques and was granted protected status in 1985 to preserve its historical significance.

Day 3: Relax at Diani Beach
Spend the day unwinding at your hotel or lounging on the beach.

Day 4: Mombasa to Tsavo East
After a hearty breakfast, depart for Tsavo East National Park, arriving in time for lunch. In the evening, enjoy a game drive before returning to the lodge or camp as the sun sets.
Tsavo East is famous for its abundant wildlife. Witness the iconic dust-red elephants of Tsavo, along with rhinos, buffalo, lions, leopards, hippos, and crocodiles. The park also features the 300-kilometre-long Yatta Plateau, the longest lava flow in the world, making it a one-of-a-kind destination.
Dinner and overnight at Voi Wildlife Lodge / Manyatta Camp (Full board).

Day 5: Tsavo West National Park
After breakfast, head to Tsavo West National Park for a full day of exploration, with a picnic lunch along the way.
Tsavo West is known for its stunning volcanic features, including Mzima Springs, where 50 million gallons of crystal-clear water emerge from beneath lava rocks. Another highlight is the Shetani Lava Flows, part of the world’s longest lava flow. The park’s rugged wilderness is home to open grasslands, scrublands, Acacia woodlands, and belts of riverine vegetation. Game viewing here is exceptional, with the chance to spot elephants, rhinos, hippos, lions, cheetahs, leopards, and a wide variety of bird species.
Dinner and overnight at Voi Wildlife Lodge / Manyatta Camp (Full board)

Day 6: Tsavo East
After breakfast, enjoy a day of game drives in Tsavo East National Park.
Dinner and overnight at Voi Wildlife Lodge / Man-eaters Camp (Full board)
Day 7 & 8: Amboseli National Park
Depart from Tsavo and head to Amboseli National Park, famous for its stunning views of Mount Kilimanjaro and its rich wildlife. Here, you’ll find large herds of elephants with some of the largest tusks in Kenya. Other wildlife includes zebras, wildebeests, giraffes, impalas, lions, cheetahs, and leopards. The park is also home to over 400 bird species, such as bee-eaters, kingfishers, and African fish eagles.
Dinner and overnight at AA Lodge (Full board)

Day 9: Return to Diani Beach
After breakfast, return to Diani Beach for a day of relaxation at your hotel.
Dinner and overnight at Jacaranda Indian Ocean Beach Resort

Day 10: Discover Gedi Ruins
After breakfast, drive north across the iconic Nyali Bridge to Kilifi and on to the village of Gede in Watamu. Spend the day exploring the Gedi Ruins, a 12th-century Swahili settlement that once thrived along the East African coast. Archaeologists have uncovered evidence of wealth and prosperity within Gedi, with intricate mosques, palaces, and homes built before the town’s mysterious abandonment in the 15th century. The site is surrounded by an ancient forest, adding to its allure.
Dinner and overnight at Jacaranda Indian Ocean Beach Resort

Day 11: Departure
Check out and transfer to the airport for your return flight home.
