Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 5-Day Tanzania Safari & Serengeti
touren.desc: Experience the Serengeti, Ngorongoro Crater, and your choice of Lake Manyara or Tarangire on our unforgettable Tanzania safari. Close up wildlife encounters, cultural experiences, and unique lodges await on this customisable adventure - Book now!
highlights: Choose between the lush, bird-filled Lake Manyara or the elephant-rich savannahs of Tarangire National Park, each offering a unique safari experience
Descend into the world-famous Ngorongoro Crater, home to the Big Five and a rare chance to see the endangered black rhinoceros in a stunning natural amphitheatre
Stay in the heart of the Serengeti’s Seronera Valley, where permanent water sources attract lions, leopards, and large herds of elephants, ensuring prime wildlife viewing year-round
Explore one of the most significant archaeological sites in the world, Oldupai Gorge, where some of the oldest evidence of early human life has been discovered
Visit an authentic Maasai village to witness traditional dance, explore local culture, and purchase handcrafted souvenirs directly from the Maasai people
itinerary:
Day 1: Ngorongoro Region – Arusha: Lake Manyara National Park or Tarangire National Park
Today, you have the option to visit either Tarangire National Park or Lake Manyara National Park. Each park offers a unique safari experience depending on the season, and your guide will recommend the best option for your visit.
If visiting Tarangire National Park:
After breakfast, you’ll depart from Arusha for the two-hour drive to Tarangire. Along the way, you’ll pass through lively Masai villages and vast open plains, iconic to African landscapes.
Upon entering Tarangire, you’ll embark on a game drive across the golden savannah surrounding the Tarangire River, a lifeline for the region’s wildlife during the dry season. The river, lined with acacia and baobab trees, attracts a large elephant population as well as zebras and antelopes.
You’ll enjoy a picnic lunch with views of the river, though be mindful of the cheeky monkeys that might try to steal your food! As you drive through the park, you’ll also have opportunities to see lions, leopards, cape buffalo, and a wide variety of antelopes. If you’re lucky, you might spot rare species like the Greater Kudu or the Fringe-Eared Oryx.
After the day’s adventures, you’ll head to your accommodation for the evening.
If visiting Lake Manyara National Park:
After breakfast, you’ll drive for two hours to Lake Manyara National Park, passing through the bustling market town of Mto Wa Mbu on the way. The market here is a vibrant mix of local cultures and offers a great opportunity for souvenir shopping.
Upon entering Lake Manyara, you’ll discover a photographer’s paradise with some of the best game viewing available. Keep an eye out for the famous tree-climbing lions that often rest in acacia trees. Bird enthusiasts will be delighted by the park’s diversity, with flamingos, birds of prey, and the colourful lilac-breasted roller among the highlights.
You’ll then retire to your accommodation for dinner and a restful night. Located in Karatu, midway between Lake Manyara and Ngorongoro, Country Lodge offers simple yet comfortable lodging, surrounded by beautiful gardens and rolling plains. This is an ideal base for exploring the Ngorongoro Crater and the surrounding area.
Overnight at Country Lodge Karatu (Full Board)

Day 2: Central Serengeti – Tarangire/Lake Manyara – Serengeti National Park
(Note: Depending on the time of year and the wildebeest migration, your accommodation within the Serengeti may vary, and the itinerary could be adjusted accordingly.)
After an early breakfast, you’ll begin the scenic drive to the Serengeti, passing through the misty rainforests of the Ngorongoro Conservation Area. Keep your eyes open for cape buffalo, baboons, and perhaps even elephants or leopards in the dense forest.
It’s a four-hour drive to the Serengeti, but you can break it up with optional visits to:
Oldupai Gorge Museum: Explore the Cradle of Mankind, where some of the oldest evidence of human habitation has been found, or
Maasai Boma: Visit a traditional Maasai village, witness a traditional dance, and browse local crafts.
After a picnic lunch in the Serengeti, it’s time for your first game drive in the world-famous park. Explore the sweeping savannah, where you’ll witness an abundance of wildlife.
Thorntree Camp is located in the Seronera Valley, known for its year-round wildlife, including lions, leopards, and large elephant herds. The camp offers stunning views of Banagi Hill and the Serengeti Plains.
Dinner and overnight in the central Serengeti at Thorntree Camp (Full Board)

Day 3: Central Serengeti – Serengeti National Park
Wake up early for a game drive at sunrise. The early morning is the best time to see predators like lions, leopards, and cheetahs as they hunt in the cooler temperatures. You’ll also witness the morning activity of herbivores like wildebeest and zebras.
Alternatively, you can opt for a sunrise hot air balloon safari, complete with a champagne breakfast. This unforgettable experience can be added to your itinerary upon request.
After a late breakfast back at the lodge, you’ll head out again with a picnic lunch, exploring the Serengeti according to the best wildlife opportunities for the season.
Overnight at Thorntree Camp (Full Board)

Day 4: Ngorongoro Crater – Serengeti National Park – Ngorongoro Conservation Area
After breakfast, you’ll set off towards the Ngorongoro Crater. Your journey takes you through the Serengeti once more, offering a second chance to spot any animals you missed on your earlier drives. Keep an eye out for cheetahs, leopards, hyenas, and the mighty lion.
You’ll stay overnight at Ngorongoro Rhino Lodge, located on the edge of the crater rim. The lodge features cosy guest rooms with wood-burning stoves, hot water bottles, and hearty meals inspired by African cuisine.
Overnight at Ngorongoro Rhino Lodge (Full Board)

Day 5: Departure – Ngorongoro Crater – Arusha
After breakfast, you’ll descend into the Ngorongoro Crater, often referred to as the Eighth Wonder of the World. This lush caldera is home to over 120 species of mammals, including the Big Five. You might spot the endangered black rhinoceros, hippos cooling off in the water, and a variety of predators such as lions, leopards, and hyenas.
After a picnic lunch by the hippo pool, you’ll begin your drive back to Arusha, concluding your unforgettable safari experience.

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 7-Day Tanzania Safari
touren.desc: Discover Tanzania’s highlights on this 7-day safari: from Tarangire’s baobabs to the Serengeti’s endless plains and the Ngorongoro Crater. Exhilarating game drives, rich wildlife encounters, and cultural experiences await. Book your Tanzania safari now!
highlights: Explore the world-renowned Serengeti, home to diverse wildlife including the Big Five, and witness the Great Migration if the timing is right
Discover the stunning Ngorongoro Crater, often referred to as the “Eighth Wonder of the World,” and encounter a high concentration of wildlife in this unique volcanic caldera
Experience the dramatic landscapes of Tarangire, with its iconic baobab trees and large herds of elephants roaming the open savannahs
Visit Lake Manyara National Park, famous for its unusual tree-climbing lions and diverse habitats, including lush rainforests and expansive lake views
Immerse yourself in the vibrant local culture of Mto wa Mbu with a traditional Tanzanian lunch and a guided tour of this diverse town
itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Tanzania
Upon arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, your driver-guide will be waiting with a sign to transfer you to your accommodation in Arusha. The approximately 90-minute drive offers great views of Mount Kilimanjaro and Mount Meru, weather permitting.
Upon reaching your lodge in Arusha, freshen up and connect with the owners, or relax by the pool or on the terrace with a Tanzanian coffee. Enjoy the sunset with your first sundowner in the Tanzanian evening light. The small guesthouse offers a friendly atmosphere with four bungalows spread across the property.
Overnight at Haradali Home

Day 2: Arusha to Tarangire National Park
After breakfast, your guide will drive you to Tarangire National Park. The journey takes around 90 minutes through small villages and scenic landscapes. Tarangire National Park Explore Tarangire’s wide panorama of open acacia forests, enormous baobab trees, and expansive grasslands. The park is renowned for its large elephant herds and diverse wildlife, including lions, cheetahs, and a variety of other mammals. Enjoy game drives throughout the day and return to your accommodation for dinner.
Drive to Karatu
Afterwards, drive to Karatu, a 90-minute journey that offers a beautiful view of Lake Manyara along the way.
Overnight at Marera Valley Lodge Located in the foothills of the Ngorongoro Highlands

Day 3: Karatu to Lake Manyara National Park
Travel from Karatu to Lake Manyara National Park in about 30 minutes. At a viewpoint, take in the scenic vistas of the park. Lake Manyara National Park, known for its diverse habitats, offers opportunities to see tree-climbing lions, a lush rainforest, and flocks of flamingos. A short hike to the hot springs is also recommended. Enjoy a private traditional Tanzanian lunch in Mto wa Mbu, where you’ll explore local life and contribute to community health initiatives.
Return to Karatu Drive back to Karatu, taking in more views of the Rift Valley.

Day 4: Ngorongoro Crater to Serengeti National Park
Drive from Karatu through the Ngorongoro Conservation Area to Serengeti National Park. Enjoy breathtaking views of the crater rim before descending into the Serengeti.
Serengeti National Park
Enter the Serengeti via Naabi Hill Gate and start your game drives in this iconic savannah. Known as the “endless land,” the Serengeti offers spectacular wildlife experiences.
Stay at the Baobab Serengeti, a tented camp with views of the Serengeti steppe, featuring international and Tanzanian cuisine, a cosy lounge, and options for bush dinners and guided walks.
Overnight at Baobab Serengeti

Day 5: Serengeti National Park
Spend a full day exploring the Serengeti’s vast landscapes and wildlife, including the Great Migration of wildebeest and zebras. Enjoy lunch in the park, and consider an optional balloon safari for an unforgettable aerial view.
Overnight at Baobab Serengeti

Day 6: Serengeti to Ngorongoro Crater
Enjoy your last game drives in the Serengeti before heading back through the Ngorongoro Conservation Area to the Ngorongoro Crater.
Ngorongoro Crater Explore the crater, known as one of the natural wonders of the world, and witness its rich wildlife and unique habitat. Return to Karatu for the night.
Optional

Day 6: Fly-Out Safari
For a more convenient departure, opt for a flight from Manyara Airstrip to your choice of airports in the late afternoon.
Overnight at Marera Valley Lodge

Day 7: Karatu to Arusha
Transfer from Karatu to Arusha or Arusha Airport, passing through small villages and experiencing the vibrant city life.

In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

An unforgettable Journey Through Uganda – The Pearl of Africa. Would return in a heartbeat.
From the moment we landed in Uganda, it was clear that this country truly lives up to its name—The Pearl of Africa. Our adventure took us through breathtaking landscapes, from the lush rainforests of Bwindi to the sprawling savannas of Queen Elizabeth National Park, and every moment was nothing short of magical.
But what made this journey truly unforgettable was our guide, Emma. From the very first day, Emma proved to be not just a guide, but the perfect travel companion—knowledgeable, patient, and incredibly passionate about his country. His expertise in geography, wildlife, and especially birds turned every drive and hike into a fascinating lesson. He went above and beyond to ensure our comfort, answered every question with a smile, and made sure we left Uganda not just with incredible memories, but with a deep appreciation for the country’s natural beauty.
If you’re planning a trip to Uganda, do yourself a favor and ask for Emma. He turned an already amazing trip into the experience of a lifetime. Uganda truly is The Pearl of Africa, and with a guide like Emma, it shines even brighter.

touren.name: 10-Day Tanzania & Zanzibar Safari
touren.desc: Set off on a thrilling gorilla trek in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park and unwind on the stunning beaches of Zanzibar. This Africa holiday package combines luxurious accommodations, cultural experiences, and diverse wildlife encounters. Unforgettable experiences await - Book now!
highlights: Encounter the rare and majestic mountain gorillas up close in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, one of the few places where these gentle giants can be observed in the wild
Relax at the Breezes Beach Club and Spa on Zanzibar, enjoying its pristine white sands, clear waters, and top-tier amenities for a perfect beach getaway
Discover the vibrant culture of Zanzibar with a spice tour, traditional dhow sailing, and visits to local markets and historic Stone Town
Experience diverse wildlife adventures, from observing gorillas in their natural habitat to spotting marine life while diving or snorkelling in the Indian Ocean
Engage with local communities, from witnessing traditional Batwa dances in Bwindi to exploring the rich cultural heritage of Zanzibar
itinerary:
Day 1: Welcome to the Former Capital of Uganda (Entebbe)
Upon your arrival at Entebbe International Airport, your guide will greet you and escort you to your guesthouse. Entebbe, a small town nestled on the shores of Lake Victoria, has a population of just 70,000. It boasts a beautiful botanical garden and offers a relaxing start to your adventure. Settle in and enjoy a refreshing traditional fruity cocktail to unwind after your journey.
Overnight at 2 Friends Beach Hotel or Karibu Guesthouse

Day 2: The Animal Kingdom of the Jungle (Entebbe, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Today, we depart for Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Known for its rich biological and botanical diversity, the park covers 331 square kilometres and is home to around 400 wild mountain gorillas—almost half of the global population of these gentle giants. Bwindi is also renowned for bird watching, internationally recognised by the African Bird Club. After a scenic drive, spend your afternoon exploring Buhoma village, visiting local handicraft markets, or learning about the Batwa tribe, an ancient people who live in the forest.
Overnight at The Haven, a community lodge in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Day 3: In Touch with the Gentle Apes (Bwindi Impenetrable National Park)
Begin your day with an early start and a hearty breakfast to prepare for gorilla tracking. After registration at the Uganda Wildlife Authority Ranger Station and a briefing, your adventure begins. The rangers will guide you through village gardens and into the dense, jungle-like forest, using machetes to clear the undergrowth as they lead the way to the gorillas, who are under 24-hour observation for their safety.
During your time with the gorillas, your guide will interpret their behaviours, offering insights into family dynamics. You’ll have the chance to observe mothers with their young, playful juveniles, and the dominant silverback. This hour-long experience with the gorillas is unforgettable, as they often display curiosity towards human visitors.
After your trek, return to the village for some well-deserved rest. In the afternoon, you can experience the Batwa tribe’s cultural performances, including their traditional dances and forest survival skills.
Overnight at The Haven
Day 4 – Relax at the Beaches of the Indian Ocean (Entebbe, Zanzibar)
After breakfast, transfer to Entebbe for your flight to Zanzibar via Dar es Salaam. Upon arrival at Zanzibar Airport, you’ll be transported to your beach resort. The island’s white sandy beaches and crystal-clear waters of the Indian Ocean create the perfect atmosphere for relaxation. A refreshing ocean breeze and a range of water sports and excursions await you at your resort.
Dinner and overnight at Breezes Beach Club and Spa
Days 5 – 9 – Relax and Explore Zanzibar (Zanzibar)
Spend the next few days soaking in the beauty of Zanzibar, a semi-autonomous part of Tanzania with a population of around 1 million. You have the freedom to choose from a wide array of activities that cater to all interests. Stroll through the hidden alleys of Stone Town for a taste of local culture or set sail on a traditional dhow for a sunset cruise. Indulge in a culinary adventure with a spice tour or sample freshly grilled seafood at Forodhani market.
For beach lovers, the surrounding islands offer exciting day trips, and if you’re lucky, you might even spot wild dolphins. For the more adventurous, options include diving in coral gardens, deep-sea fishing, or windsurfing. Zanzibar’s diverse experiences promise something for everyone.
Dinner and overnight at Breezes Beach Club and Spa
Day 10 – Goodbye (Zanzibar)
After breakfast, transfer from your resort to Zanzibar Airport, according to your departure schedule. As you bid farewell to this unforgettable adventure, you’ll carry with you lasting memories of Africa’s beauty. We hope to welcome you back to East Africa soon!
Before Booking, Please Read the Following Carefully
Prices are valid for 2024, 2025 and 2026.
The itinerary mentioned for this tour is an example which can be tailored to your wishes.
The lodges and hotels are subject to availability at the time of booking. In the event that a property is unavailable, we will be pleased to offer a suitable alternative of a similar range, quality, and service standard to ensure your comfort and satisfaction.
The Gorilla Tracking included in this safari depends on the availability of gorilla trekking permits at the time of booking. These permits are strictly limited and sold out in advance, especially during peak seasons of Christmas and school holidays. We therefore strongly recommend booking early.
The safari indicated here is essentially an offer. Changes in the course of the journey, the duration of your tour, the number of travellers, as well as the choice of accommodation are subject to availability, although we always endeavour to stick as closely as possible to this programme. For most itineraries, we can offer luxury, mid-range or budget versions of this safari upon request.
Sometimes it is not possible to adhere to the travel plan as described in this itinerary, due to weather and road conditions, authority regulations, organisational requirements, etc. Occasionally, the planned route of the tour has to change according to the situation on the ground. We must therefore explicitly refuse liability for adherence to the journey plan.

touren.name: 12-Day Luxury Tanzania & Kenya Safari
touren.desc: Visit Tanzania and Kenya on our epic East African safari! From the breathtaking Ngorongoro Crater and Serengeti plains to the tree-climbing lions of Lake Manyara and the iconic views of Amboseli and Masai Mara. Discover unparalleled wildlife and stunning landscapes.
highlights: Descend into the Ngorongoro Crater to see a rich variety of wildlife, from forest monkeys and elephants to flamingos and lions
Explore the vast plains of Serengeti National Park, known for its spectacular game viewing, including predators like lions and cheetahs
Witness the unique tree-climbing lions of Lake Manyara National Park, along with giraffes, zebras, and diverse bird species
Marvel at Mount Kilimanjaro’s views in Amboseli National Park while tracking large herds of elephants and spotting other wildlife
Enjoy game drives in the Masai Mara Game Reserve, famous for its abundant wildlife and the chance to see the Big Five
Discover the Pink Paradise of Lake Nakuru, a bird lover’s dream, famous for its vast flocks of flamingos that turn the lake’s surface a mesmerizing pink
itinerary:
Day 1: Nairobi
On arrival, you will be met and transferred to Southern Sun Mayfair for a welcome dinner and overnight stay.
Overnight: Southern Sun Mayfair or similar (B)

Day 2: Nairobi to Arusha to Ngorongoro Crater
After breakfast, your guide will pick you up and transfer you across the Kenya/Tanzania border to Arusha for lunch. Following lunch, drive to Ngorongoro Conservation Area and check in at your lodge in Karatu.
Dinner and Overnight: Ngorongoro Farm House (Full Board)

Day 3: Ngorongoro Crater
Descend 600 metres into the Ngorongoro Crater for a day tour. Explore the forested areas, lake region with flamingos, and open savannah where lions hunt. Enjoy a picnic lunch at a scenic spot within the crater. Return to the lodge in the afternoon.
Dinner and Overnight: Ngorongoro Farm House (Full Board)

Day 4: Ngorongoro Crater to Serengeti National Park
After breakfast, check out and drive to Serengeti National Park, arriving in time for lunch at your lodge. Enjoy an afternoon game drive in the park.
Dinner and Overnight: Serengeti Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 5: Serengeti National Park
Spend the day exploring Serengeti National Park. To maximise game viewing, consider a picnic lunch and spend the full day in the park, home to a wide variety of African wildlife, including lions, leopards, cheetahs, and their prey.
Dinner and Overnight: Serengeti Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 6: Serengeti to Lake Manyara National Park
After breakfast and check-out, drive to Lake Manyara, a soda lake in the Rift Valley. Enjoy lunch at Kirurumu Tented Lodge, then take an afternoon game drive in Lake Manyara National Park. Explore diverse landscapes and spot wildlife such as giraffes, zebras, elephants, and tree-climbing lions.
Dinner and Overnight: Kirurumu Tented Lodge (Full Board)

Day 7: Lake Manyara to Amboseli National Park
Drive to Arusha for lunch, then continue to the Kenya/Tanzania border at Namanga. After clearing border formalities, proceed to Amboseli National Park. Check in at your lodge and enjoy an en route game drive.
Dinner and Overnight: Amboseli Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 8: Amboseli National Park
Admire stunning views of snow-capped Mount Kilimanjaro from the park. Amboseli is known for its large herds of elephants and offers excellent game drives to spot lions, cheetahs, wildebeests, and the rare black rhino.
All Meals and Overnight: Amboseli Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 9: Amboseli to Lake Nakuru National Park
After breakfast, drive to Lake Nakuru National Park, with a picnic lunch provided en route. Check in and enjoy an evening game drive in the park.
Dinner and Overnight: Lake Nakuru Lodge or similar (Full Board)

Day 10: Lake Nakuru to Masai Mara Game Reserve
Drive via Narok to Maasai Mara Game Reserve. Arrive for lunch and an afternoon game drive in the reserve.
Dinner and Overnight: Mara Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 11: Masai Mara Game Reserve
Spend a full day in the Maasai Mara with morning and afternoon game drives. The reserve is renowned for its abundant wildlife, including the Big Five and numerous other species such as cheetahs, giraffes, and wildebeests.
Dinner and Overnight: Mara Sopa Lodge (Full Board)

Day 12: Departure
After breakfast, drive back to Nairobi, arriving around midday. Transfer to your Nairobi hotel or Jomo Kenyatta International Airport.

Rhinos, Giraffes, Elephants, Birds, and Memories: Manya Africa Tours Knows Uganda's Murchison Falls National Park
Are you looking to create lifelong memories on an upcoming trip to Africa? Manya Africa Tours will more than meet your expectations! Our team took a three-day safari adventure from Kampala to Murchison Falls from October 17-19. Our driver and guide for the three days was Emma (short for Emmanuel). Emma met us at our Kampala accommodations early, surprised us with travel gifts, loaded us up and took off to make the most of our day. First, let me say -- if you are a birder (as my wife and I are), then be sure to ask for Emma. The man knows his birds!! In three short days we added nearly a hundred new species to our life lists.
Our first stop was at the Ziwa Rhino Preserve to see Southern White Rhinos. After a brief safety talk and then a short walk with two rangers into the brush, we came upon the rhino named Nguzo (Ki-Swahili for "Pillar"), who was settling down for a mid-day nap. We circled around Nguzo and found ourselves beside a crash of six or seven rhinos that included a couple of little ones. Ziwa Preserve should definitely be on your bucket list.
We arrived at Murchison Falls National Park as twilight was setting in, but it didn't stop Emma from pointing out the primates along the way, as well as several bird species, including a good look at a Black-and-white-casqued Hornbill. We arrived at our accommodations at the Murchison River Lodge perched alongside the Nile River. What an amazing adventure it was simply to stay at the lodge, dine alongside the Nile, and listen to birds and wildlife wake us up in the morning. The food and service at the Lodge was excellent.
Right after an early breakfast, Emma loaded us into the very accommodating, comfortable safari vehicle with a pop-top roof that enabled all of us to stand up and observe the animals of the day. We saw variety of bird species (including my personal favorites: an African Pygmy Kingfisher, a Grey-headed Kingfisher, a Northern Red Bishop, and Grey Hornbills). In the course of the morning, we saw such game as cape buffalo, plenty of giraffe, elephants, hartebeests, Uganda Kob, warthog, dik-dik, bushbuck, and more. We were unable to find the big cats, but apparently none of the other guides out that morning were able to find them either. Still, the morning was magnificent.
Later that afternoon, after a satisfying lunch, Emma negotiated a private boat for us to safari up the Nile to the base of Murchison Falls. Our captain and guide for the trip was Saad. The 17 kilometer journey (one way) brought us by several hippo herds a few crocodiles sunning on little islands near the shoreline, and a couple memories of elephants. We were put on to several species of shorebirds, including a Goliath Heron (the world's largest heron), African Fish Eagle, and African Skimmers.
At dusk, we returned to our lodge, enjoyed a few drinks, and slept through the night in our mid-ranged priced thatched safari tents. Personally, I just loved the experience of sleeping in the safari tent. It's not for those who demand extra comfort, such as air conditioning or indoor plumbing. Still, the cots are comfortable, there are a couple of electrical outlets to charge up electronics. The facilities are attached to the back of the tent and have a thatched roofed. They are clean; the showers have hot water, and the toilet is a flush toilet. What more could you ask for in a site near the Nile River?
On our final morning of the safari, we headed to the top of Murchison Falls after a breakfast at the lodge. In addition, the lodge packed us a take-away lunch for our trip back to Kampala. At the top of the falls, Emma remained behind to hold on to valuable camera equipment which otherwise would have been soaked by the mist being generated by the magnificent, but powerful falls. That the entire Nile River squeezes through such a narrow crevice is incredible. Words, pictures, and even video can never do such power justice. This is one vista that you must experience to appreciate.
In all, this was a five-star experience. Emma was the best guide that we could have hoped for. Manya Tours took exceptional care of us. I will definitely use them again when I return to Uganda. Thayou, Emma and Manya Africa Tours, for an unforgettable adventure!

touren.name: 14-Day Uganda & Rwanda combined Luxury Safari
touren.desc: Comprehensive luxury safari combining Uganda and Rwanda. Gorilla trekking in Bwindi & Volcanoes NP, chimpanzee tracking in Kibale, Murchison Falls, Queen Elizabeth NP, Lake Bunyonyi and Lake Mburo.
highlights: Gorilla-Trekking Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
Gorilla-Trekking Parc National des Volcans (Ruanda)
Schimpansen-Tracking Kibale Forest
Murchison Falls – Bootsafari auf dem Nil
Queen Elizabeth NP – Kazinga-Kanal Bootsafari
Lake Bunyonyi – Inselwelt & Kanufahren
Lake Mburo – Walking Safari & Zebras
Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary
Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary
Kulturbesuch Familie Tinka John & Iby'iwacu
itinerary: Tag 1 – Ankunft Entebbe: Bei Ihrer Ankunft am Entebbe International Airport werden Sie von Ihrem Manya Africa Tours Guide empfangen und ins Hotel transferiert (15–40 Min.). Overnight: The Boma (BB).

Tag 2 – Murchison Falls Nationalpark: Fahrt zum Murchison Falls NP. Halt am Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary – einziger Ort in Uganda mit Nashörnern. Der Nil zieht Nilkrokodile, Flusspferde und Sitatunga-Antilopen an. Löwen, Leoparden, Elefanten, Büffel, Rothschild-Giraffen, Warzenschweine, Riesenwaldscheine, Uganda-Kobs, Topis und Wasserböcke. Optional: Besuch Top of the Falls (Nil verengt sich auf 7m, 45m Sturz). Overnight: Paraa Lodge (VP).

Tag 3 – Bootsafari auf dem Nil: Frühe Pirschfahrt am Morgen – optional Frühstückspaket mitnehmen. Einer der Höhepunkte der Safari: Bootsfahrt zum Fuß der Murchison Falls. Overnight: Paraa Safari Lodge (VP).

Tag 4 – Kibale Forest Nationalpark: Fahrt über Hoima zum Kibale Forest NP (795 km²). Heimat von 335+ Vogelarten und 13 Primatenarten: Schimpansen, Roter Stummelaffe, L'Hoest-Meerkatze, Blaue Meerkatze, Graue Mangabe, Schwarzgesicht-Vervetaffe, Paviane, Potto und Buschbabys. Zwischenstopp an einer Teeplantage. Kibale Primate Lodge: nächstgelegene Lodge zum Schimpansen-Trekking. Overnight: Kibale Primate Lodge (VP).

Tag 5 – Schimpansen-Tracking im Dschungel: Um 8 Uhr Anmeldung an der Rangerwache des Kibale Forest NP. Erfahrener Ranger stellt Schimpansenfamilie vor. Waldspaziergang – Nutzung von Waldpflanzen durch Einheimische. Auch Waldelefanten, Büffel, Waldschweins und Antilopen möglich. Mittagessen bei Familie Tinka John – Einblick in Kulturen und Küche der Region. Nachmittags: Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary mit 130+ Vogelarten, 8 Primatenarten, seltener Sitatunga-Antilope. Overnight: Primate Lodge (VP).

Tag 6 – Queen Elizabeth Nationalpark: Fahrt durch Kasese zum Queen Elizabeth NP am Fuß des Rift Valley. Gelegentlich Gipfel der Rwenzori-Berge am Horizont sichtbar. 600+ Vogelarten. Unterkunft hoch auf dem Kichwamba-Kamm mit Panoramablick über Rift Valley, Rwenzori-Ausläufer und acht Virunga-Vulkane. Overnight: Mweya oder Katara Lodge (VP).

Tag 7 – Pirschfahrt & Bootsafari Queen Elizabeth NP: 6-Uhr-Weckruf. Frühe Pirschfahrt im Kasenyi-Sektor – bekannt für Löwen die Uganda-Kobs bejagen. Wildlife: Elefanten, Antilopen, Warzenschweine, Paviane, gelegentlich Hyänen, Riesenwaldschwein. Nachmittags Bootsafari auf dem Kazinga-Kanal (verbindet Seen Edward und George). 100 Kanalvogelarten: Afrikanische Scherenschnäbel, Große Weißpelikane, Löffelreiher, Bienenfresser. Flusspferde, Nilkrokodile, Elefanten säumen die Ufer. Overnight: Mweya Safari Lodge oder Katara Lodge (VP).

Tag 8 – Trekking Kyambura-Schlucht: Wanderung durch Kyambura-Schlucht am Rand des QENP. Schluchtwald: diverse Vogelwelt, kleine Schimpansengruppe, Schwarz-Weiß-Colobus und Vervetaffen. Beim Überqueren des Schluchtflusses: Flusspferde, Elefanten und Antilopen. Optional: Höhlenbesuch bei den Zwillingsseen. Overnight: Mweya Safari Lodge oder Katara Lodge (VP).

Tag 9 – Abreise nach Ruanda: Morgens Abfahrt Richtung Süden zur ugandisch-ruandischen Grenze. Bei Kabale erster Blick auf den Virunga-Komplex. Grenzübertritt nach Ruanda. Bald Parc National des Volcans – Vorbereitung auf die Gorilla-Trekkings der Folgetage in atemberaubender Landschaft. Overnight: Gorilla Nest Lodge (VP).

Tag 10 – Gorilla-Trekking Parc National des Volcans: Frühes Aufstehen. Um 8 Uhr Anmeldung an der Rangerstation der Uganda Wildlife Authority. Einweisung durch Ranger, dann Beginn des Treks (3–6 Stunden, gute Fitness erforderlich). Ranger führen durch Dorfgärten und Dschungel. Berggorillas (Gorilla beringei beringei) stehen unter 24-Stunden-Überwachung – Sichtung nahezu garantiert. Mindestabstand 7m einhalten. Optional: Kulturerdorf Iby'iwacu – Tanz, Medizinmann, Königshaus. Overnight: Gorilla Nest Lodge (VP).

Tag 11 – Lake Bunyonyi: Rückkehr nach Uganda, Fahrt zum Lake Bunyonyi – See mit 22 Inseln, prachtvoller Vogelwelt, Fischottern. Optional: Kanufahren und Community-Tourismus. Picknick-Mittagessen unterwegs. Nachmittags Spaziergang mit Panoramafotografie oder Naturwanderung. Overnight: Bird's Nest (VP).

Tag 12 – Lake Mburo Nationalpark: Früh von Lake Bunyonyi nach Lake Mburo durch Kabale 'Gorilla Highlands' (Schweizer der Afrikas, bis 2.347m). Lake Mburo: kleinster Savannen-NP Ugandas, heiß und trocken. Mihingo Lodge: auf Felsen über Wasserloch. Naturwanderungen zu Giraffen und Steppenzebras, Mountainbike-Touren, Infinity-Pool. Overnight: Mihingo Lodge (VP).

Tag 13 – Lake Mburo Nationalpark: Walking Safari mit 350 Vogelarten, Impalas, Elenantilopen, Büffel, Oribi, Defassa-Wasserböcke, Leoparden, Flusspferde, Hyänen, Topis, Riedbocke. Steppenzebras als auffälligste Bewohner; kürzlich umgesiedelte Nubische Giraffen aus Murchison Falls. Wahl zwischen Frühmorgenwanderung oder Nachmittags-Bootsfahrt auf dem See. Optional: Nächtliche Pirschfahrt – sehr empfehlenswert! Overnight: Mihingo Lodge (VP).

Tag 14 – Abreise Entebbe: Nach Frühstück Rückfahrt zum Entebbe International Airport mit Picknick-Mittagessen und Fotostopp am Äquator. Guide begleitet zur letzten Übernachtung in Entebbe. Nach unvergesslichem Abschiedsessen wünscht der Guide eine gute Heimreise.

touren.name: 10 Days around Mount Kenya
touren.desc: This 10-day Mt. Kenya safari takes you through some of Kenya's most scenic and wildlife-rich areas. Highlights include thrilling water activities in Sagana, game drives in the remote and lush Meru National Park, and exploring the unique wildlife of Samburu Game Reserve. Experience the natural beauty of Ngare Ndare Forest, engage with Maasai culture at Twala Tenebo, and visit Ol Pejeta Conservancy, home to endangered rhinos and the Sweetwaters Chimpanzee Sanctuary. The safari offers a mix of adventure, wildlife, and cultural immersion, all set against stunning Kenyan landscapes.
highlights: Game drives and park fees according to the itinerary.
Mineral water during game drives.
We are taking a decisive step toward a sustainable future, where every safari journey contributes to preserving the environment for future generations.
itinerary: Nairobi to Sagana:: Begin your adventure with an early morning drive from Nairobi to Sagana, a small town in Central Kenya known for its hospitality industry along the Sagana River. Enjoy outdoor activities like zip-lining, kayaking, or rafting on this day. Stay at Gateway Resort (FB).

Sagana to Meru National Park:: Embark on a scenic drive across the Central Highlands, passing farmlands with pineapples, coffee, and rice. Cross the mighty Tana River and descend towards Meru National Park. After lunch, enjoy a game drive in this unspoilt park, home to zebras, elephants, and over 427 bird species. Stay at Ikweta Safari Camp (FB).

Meru National Park:: Spend a full day exploring the lush landscapes of Meru National Park. Enjoy a picnic lunch during the game drive, where you’ll discover rivers, swamps, and a variety of wildlife in a rugged, remote environment. Overnight at Ikweta Safari Camp (FB).

Meru to Samburu:: Drive to Samburu Game Reserve, a unique region north of the equator. Experience the scenic plains and woodlands, home to diverse species like giraffes, zebras, elephants, and lions. An afternoon game drive offers spectacular wildlife viewing. Stay at Samburu Intrepids (FB).

Samburu Game Reserve:: Enjoy a full day in Samburu, where the Ewaso Ng’iro River supports rich wildlife, including the graceful giraffe gazelle. With over 380 bird species, this is a haven for birdwatching enthusiasts. Overnight at Samburu Intrepids (FB).

Ngare Ndare Forest:: Travel to the stunning Ngare Ndare Forest, located at the foothills of Mt. Kenya. Walk across East Africa’s longest suspension bridge, offering breathtaking views of elephants and other wildlife. End the day with a hike to the azure poolsand waterfalls, where you can swim in the natural beauty. Overnight at Le Rustique (FB).

Twala Tenebo (Laikipia Plateau):: Visit the Twala Tenebo Women’s Cooperative, where over 200 Maasai women sustain themselves through aloe farming, beekeeping, and ecotourism. Learn about their cultural practices, participate in a baboon walk, and explore the medicinal plant landscape tours with the Maasai. Overnight at Le Rustique (FB). Scenic Nature and Cultural Experiences Conservation and Wildlife Encounters

Ol Pejeta Conservancy:: Head to Ol Pejeta Conservancy, home to endangered species like black rhinos and chimpanzees. Take part in optional activities like bird walks, horseback riding, or camel rides, and witness Africa’s big cats during game drives. Stay at Serena Sweetwaters (FB).

Ol Pejeta Conservancy:: Continue exploring Ol Pejeta’s rhino sanctuary, home to the last two northern white rhinos. Visit the Sweetwaters Chimpanzee Sanctuary and enjoy a day filled with conservation stories, observing a variety of predators and large mammals. Overnight at Serena Sweetwaters (FB).

Ol Pejeta to Nairobi:: After breakfast, enjoy a final game drive before heading back to Nairobi for your departure flight. Start your trip here Adventure Expeditions Logo We always aim to reply within 24 hours. touren.name: 7 Days Great Rift Bike Safari
touren.desc: Embark on an exhilarating 7-day adventure, starting with a scenic bike ride from Ngong Hills to Lake Naivasha, where you’ll overnight at Crescent Camp. The next day, explore Hells Gate National Park, followed by a visit to Crescent Island. On Day 3, ride through the stunning Eburru Mountains to reach Lake Elementaita for a camping experience. Day 4 features a wildlife safari at Lake Nakuru National Park before heading to Lake Bogoria Resort & Spa for a restful night. Enjoy bird watching and cultural walks around Lake Bogoria on Day 5. On Day 6, indulge in water sports at Lake Baringo after a delightful lunch at Lake Baringo Club, then relax at Soi Safari Lodge. Finally, on Day 7, return to Nairobi, enjoying a lunch stop along the way to conclude your unforgettable journey.
highlights: 7 Days Great Rift Bike Safari Highlight
Park Entrance Fees to respective National parks/Game Reserve

itinerary: Morning: Arrival and Transfer Pick Up: Upon arrival at the airport, you will be greeted by our representative and transferred to Ngong Hills by vehicle. Refreshments: Enjoy light refreshments during the transfer. Midday: Cycling at Ngong Hills Start Your Ride: Begin your cycling adventure at Ngong Hills, known for its stunning views and cool climate. Support Vehicle: A support vehicle equipped with refreshments, first aid supplies, luggage, and a bike trailer will accompany you throughout the ride for your convenience. Afternoon: Ride to Lake Naivasha Lunch: Stop for a hearty lunch en route to Lake Naivasha. Crescent Camp: Arrive at Crescent Camp, where you’ll check in for your overnight stay. Evening: Relaxation at the Camp Dinner: Enjoy dinner at the camp. Accommodation: Overnight stay at Crescent Camp. Meal Plan: Full Board (FB). Ride to Hell’s Gate: Cycle to Hell’s Gate National Park, where you can walk and ride while viewing wildlife, including zebras, giraffes, and antelopes. Midday: Exploring Ol Njorowa Gorge Gorge Walk: Take a guided walk into the Ol Njorowa Gorge, a beautiful site with stunning rock formations and wildlife. Return to Camp: Cycle back to Crescent Camp for lunch. touren.name: 7 Days Best of Birding Safari
touren.desc: This seven-day itinerary begins with a scenic drive from Nairobi to Lake Baringo, a birdwatcher's paradise with over 460 bird species and hippos, including an ornithologist-led walk. Day two includes a boat trip on Lake Baringo and an excursion to Lake Bogoria, famous for its flamingos, hot springs, and geysers. On day three, you'll journey to Kakamega Forest, Kenya's only tropical rainforest, home to rare birds and exotic wildlife. Day four offers a full day exploring Kakamega Forest, followed by a drive to Lake Nakuru on day five, known for its flamingos, rhinos, and diverse birdlife. On day six, you'll visit Hell’s Gate National Park and Lake Naivasha, with optional boat rides and bird-watching. The trip concludes with a leisurely return to Nairobi on day seven.
highlights: 7 Days Best of Birding Safari Highlight
Park Entrance Fees to respective National parks/Game Reserve
Game viewing drives per day whilst on safari
itinerary: Nairobi to Lake Baringo:: Start your safari with a scenic drive from Nairobi through the lush Limuru tea farms, descending into the Great Rift Valley. Along the way, take in breathtaking views of Mt. Longonot and Lake Naivasha from a viewpoint before continuing to Lake Baringo. This freshwater lake is a paradise for birdwatchers, with over 460 bird species recorded, including hippos along the shores. A resident ornithologist will guide you on bird walks in the morning and evening. Spend the night at Tumbili Cliff with a full board meal plan.

Lake Baringo and Lake Bogoria:: Begin the day with a 1-hour boat ride on Lake Baringo, where you’ll spot more bird species and hippos. Optional excursions to the Njemp village to learn about local culture or a snake farm are available for an additional cost. Afterward, travel to Lake Bogoria, known for its soda-rich waters that attract thousands of pink flamingos. Experience the natural beauty of the lake with geysers and hot springs at its southern end. Enjoy dinner and an overnight stay at Tumbili Cliff with a full board meal plan.

Lake Baringo to Kakamega Forest National Reserve:: After breakfast, depart for Kakamega Forest National Reserve, Kenya’s only tropical rainforest and part of a once vast forest that stretched across Central and West Africa. This unique ecosystem is renowned for its biodiversity, including rare snake-eating birds, various insects, primates, and exotic tree species. Upon arrival, explore the lush surroundings before enjoying dinner and overnight accommodation at the Rondo Retreat or a similar lodge/campsite, with a full board meal plan.

Full Day in Kakamega Forest National Reserve:: Spend the entire day immersed in Kakamega Forest’s natural beauty. Enjoy guided bird walks led by game wardens and expert guides as you discover hundreds of bird species, vibrant wildlife, and diverse flora. This day is perfect for birdwatchers and nature lovers who seek tranquility in a rare tropical rainforest. In the evening, relax and enjoy dinner before settling in for another night at Rondo Retreat or a similar lodge/campsite. Kakamega Forest National Reserve Lake Nakuru, Hell’s Gate, and Naivasha

Kakamega to Lake Nakuru National Park:: Drive to Lake Nakuru National Park after an early breakfast. The park is famous for its large flocks of pink flamingos, which color the lake’s shores, and offers sightings of white pelicans, marabou storks, and other water birds. It is also home to a variety of wildlife, including white rhinos and the rare Rothschild giraffe. After lunch at Lake Nakuru Lodge, enjoy an afternoon game drive before dinner and an overnight stay at the lodge.

Lake Nakuru to Naivasha via Hell’s Gate:: Before breakfast, go on a bird-watching drive around Lake Nakuru to spot more bird species and wildlife. Continue to Hell’s Gate National Park, famous for its natural geysers and diverse birdlife, where you can enjoy hiking or walking while observing open vultures, eagles, and swifts. After a stop at the Lake Elementaita viewpoint, have lunch at Lake Naivasha Country Club, and spend the afternoon at leisure exploring bird-filled gardens or opt for a boat ride to Crescent Island (extra cost). Dinner and overnight will be at the Lake Naivasha Country Club or similar.

Naivasha to Nairobi:: After a leisurely breakfast, return to Nairobi, marking the end of your safari. You’ll either be dropped off at your hotel or at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport for your outbound flight. Start your trip here Adventure Expeditions Logo We always aim to reply within 24 hours. touren.name: 7 Days Lake Turkana Safari
touren.desc: This seven-day adventure begins with a flight from Nairobi to Lodwar, followed by a visit to Eliye Springs on the shores of Lake Turkana. You’ll explore a local fishing village, learn about traditional Turkana fishing, and visit the monument of the Turkana Boy, one of the world’s most famous hominid fossils. A boat ride to Central Island National Park offers a chance to explore volcanic landscapes and enjoy bird watching. Relaxation at the beach and cultural experiences, such as a "Turkana Night" with traditional dances, add to the itinerary. The journey concludes with a visit to the El Molo tribe, a boat trip, and a return flight to Nairobi.
highlights: 7 Days Lake Turkana Safari Highlights
Park Entrance Fees to respective National parks/Game Reserve
Game viewing drives per day whilst on safari
itinerary: Nairobi to Lodwar, Eliye Springs, Lake Turkana:: Start your journey with a morning flight from Nairobi to Lodwar. Upon arrival, you will be met by a representative and driven to Eliye Springs, a serene retreat on the shores of Lake Turkana. Spend your first few hours relaxing at the beach, soaking in the tranquility of this remote paradise. In the late afternoon, visit the fishing village of Longelech where you’ll witness traditional “shore” fishing techniques practiced by the Turkana people. Return to your lodge for a delightful dinner and overnight stay, immersed in the local culture.

Nariokotome (Turkana Boy):: After an early breakfast, head north along the lake to explore the cultural and historical treasures of the Turkana region. Visit a traditional Turkana village and experience their unique way of life. Continue to the famous Nariokotome excavation site where the Turkana Boy, a nearly complete hominid skeleton, was discovered. Learn from local guides about the importance of this landmark archaeological find. After a fulfilling day, return to Eliye Springs for dinner and a restful overnight.

Central Island National Park:: Set out on an exciting morning adventure after breakfast with a motorboat ride to Central Island National Park, one of Lake Turkana’s three volcanic islands. Explore the island’s unique wildlife, stunning volcanic landscapes, and enjoy a picnic lunch surrounded by the pristine environment. Return to Eliye Springs in the afternoon, or for the more adventurous, spend the evening under the stars on Central Island in a “star bed” for an unforgettable nature experience. This optional overnight offers an incredible opportunity to connect with nature in one of Africa’s most remote locations.

Beach Day at Eliye Springs:: After breakfast, enjoy a leisurely day at Eliye Springs. You can either take a morning bird-watching tour on Central Island before returning by boat or relax on the sandy beach. The day is yours to unwind and immerse in the serenity of Lake Turkana’s shores. In the evening, the resort hosts a special “Turkana Night” with traditional dances and songs around the campfire, providing a glimpse into the rich cultural heritage of the Turkana people. Adventure at Central Island National Park and Eliye Springs Exploring Moite, El Molo Tribe, and Departure

Moite and El Molo Tribe Exploration:: Start your day with a boat transfer to the eastern shores of Lake Turkana, heading towards the Moite region. This area is home to both the Turkana and the El Molo tribes, the latter being one of Kenya’s smallest and most endangered ethnic groups. Spend the afternoon learning about their unique customs and way of life. In the evening, return to Eliye Springs for dinner and overnight.

Eliye Springs Beach Day:: Spend your final full day in Eliye Springs enjoying the lake, beach, and sun. For a more relaxing experience, you can opt for a massage at the resort or take part in a fishing trip. End the day with a beautiful boat trip at sunset, accompanied by a Sundowner to mark the perfect conclusion to your Lake Turkana adventure.

Departure:: After a final breakfast by the beach, transfer back to Lodwar for your flight to Nairobi, bringing your Turkana adventure to an end. touren.name: 13 Days Kenya Historic Railway
touren.desc: This 13-day itinerary offers an enriching exploration of Kenya's historic, cultural, and natural wonders. Starting in Nairobi, you'll visit the Nairobi Railway Museum, tracing the early railway history before heading to Tsavo, renowned for its legendary man-eating lions. The journey continues with an unforgettable game drive in Tsavo East, where you'll witness abundant wildlife, including dust-red elephants and hippos. Afterward, relax on Mombasa’s Bamburi Beach and explore Fort Jesus and Old Town's historical architecture. The tour ends with an excursion to the lush Nandi Hills, Lake Naivasha, Lake Nakuru, and Kisumu, blending Kenya's scenic landscapes, colonial history, and unique wildlife.
highlights: Game drives and park fees according to the itinerary.
mineral water during game drives.
We are taking a decisive step toward a sustainable future, where every safari journey contributes to preserving the environment for future generations.
itinerary: Arrival in Nairobi Upon arrival at the Jomo Kenyatta International Airport in Nairobi, you will be picked up and transferred to the Trademark Hotel. After a brief rest, your first adventure begins with a visit to the Nairobi Railway Museum, a historical site that delves into Kenya’s colonial railway history dating back to 1896. You’ll discover captivating exhibits, including early steam engines and the notorious motorized railway bicycle. Highlights include the impressive “Garratts” steam engine, famed for its power in hauling heavy loads across difficult terrain.::

Nairobi to Tsavo After an early breakfast, you will embark on a journey heading east out of Nairobi. Your first stop is at Athi River Station (built in 1920) for a quick break before continuing to Konza and Emali stations. The drive offers breathtaking views of the Athi Plains, echoing the journey of early travelers aboard the “Lunatic Express.” On arrival at Tsavo Station, you will pause to reflect on the station’s history, known for the infamous Tsavo man-eaters—lions that attacked railway workers in the late 19th century. Dinner and overnight stay will be at Maneaters Camp.::

Tsavo East National Park A day filled with wildlife adventures begins with breakfast, followed by a visit to Tsavo East National Park, home to iconic African wildlife such as dust-red elephants, rhinos, buffalo, lions, and crocodiles. You will witness the impressive Yatta Plateau, the world’s longest lava flow, adding to Tsavo’s wild landscape. With over 500 bird species and scenic views along the Galana River, the park offers an unforgettable safari experience. After an evening game drive, you will return to Maneaters Camp for dinner and an overnight stay.::

Tsavo to Mombasa Following breakfast, you’ll leave Tsavo and take a four-hour drive to Mombasa, Kenya’s coastal city. Upon arrival, you will check in at Bamburi Beach Hotel and spend the rest of the day relaxing along the Indian Ocean coastline.::

Mombasa – Fort Jesus and Old Town Start the day with breakfast by the ocean, followed by an excursion to Fort Jesus, a UNESCO World Heritage site built by the Portuguese in the late 16th century. Explore the fort’s architecture, including remnants of old battlements, Omani houses, and the on-site museum housing artifacts that tell the tale of Mombasa’s trading history. Afterward, a tour of Mombasa Old Town takes you through narrow streets lined with ancient buildings, intricate Swahili door carvings, and Islamic architecture. Dinner and overnight will be at Bamburi Beach Hotel.::

Mombasa to Nairobi via SGR An early morning journey on the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Mombasa to Nairobi offers scenic views of Tsavo National Park once more, allowing you to reminisce about your safari experience. The ride also takes you past stations visited earlier on the tour. You can end the tour in Nairobi, or extend it for more exploration.:: Mombasa and Coastal Exploration Days 7 - 13 Rift Valley Lakes and Western Kenya (Optional Extension)

Nairobi to Lake Naivasha For those extending their tour, your driver will meet you at the Nairobi train station and take you to Lake Naivasha, located in the Rift Valley. Enjoy a relaxing boat ride to Crescent Island, a unique wildlife sanctuary. On the island, you can encounter giraffes, hippos, gazelles, and various birds. After the tour, return to Lake Naivasha Country Club for lunch and leisure. Overnight stay at Crescent Camp.::

Lake Naivasha to Lake Nakuru After breakfast, travel to Lake Nakuru National Park, home to flamingos, waterbucks, and a variety of large mammals such as lions, buffalos, and rhinos. Following a thrilling afternoon game drive, relax at Flamingo Hill Camp with dinner and overnight accommodations.::

Lake Nakuru to Koru Today’s journey takes you westward to Koru, stopping at stations such as Elburgon and Londiani. At Fort Tenan, admire historic railway bridges before arriving at Kweisos House, a charming farmhouse known for traditional cuisine and comfort. Spend the afternoon enjoying the peaceful rural surroundings.::

Koru to Kapsimotwa Gardens A serene day at Kapsimotwa Gardens awaits, where you will tour this lush highland farm known for its tea plantations. The Nandi Hills region, rich in history and natural beauty, was also the site of resistance against British rule, led by the famed Koitalel Arap Samoei.::

Koru to Kisumu After breakfast, you’ll journey to Kisumu, a vibrant city located on the shores of Lake Victoria. As you drive through Kenya’s sugar plantations, you’ll learn about the city’s origins as a major terminal for the Kenya-Uganda Railway, marking its historical importance. Enjoy lunch and leisure time at your hotel in Kisumu.::

Kisumu to Kakamega Forest The morning brings you to the Kakamega Forest, a remnant of the once extensive Central African rainforest. This biologically rich area is home to various primates, bird species, and wildlife, making it a haven for nature enthusiasts. After a full day exploring the forest, return to your hotel in Kisumu for the night.::

Kisumu to Nairobi The tour concludes with a six-hour drive back to Nairobi, where you’ll be transferred to the airport for your flight home, completing your incredible Kenya adventure.:: Start your trip here Adventure Expeditions Logo We always aim to reply within 24 hours. touren.name: 14 Days Kenya Rift Valley Lakes
touren.desc: This 14-day safari adventure begins in Nairobi, taking you through some of Kenya's most iconic lakes and landscapes. You'll explore the rich biodiversity of Lake Magadi, Lake Naivasha, and Lake Nakuru, with opportunities to see flamingos, giraffes, hippos, rhinos, and a variety of bird species. The itinerary includes visits to archaeological sites, the Masai Mara region, and the breathtaking Rift Valley, as well as cultural experiences with the Maasai and Turkana tribes. Highlights include Crescent Island, Lake Bogoria's geysers, and a trip to Central Island on Lake Turkana. The journey ends with relaxation at Eliye Springs before returning to Nairobi.
highlights: Game drives and park fees according to the itinerary.
Mineral water during game drives.
We are taking a decisive step toward a sustainable future, where every safari journey contributes to preserving the environment for future generations.
itinerary: Upon arrival in Nairobi, guests are picked up from the airport and taken to Trademark Hotel for an overnight stay. The day is spent relaxing in preparation for the safari.::

Begin the safari at Lake Magadi, a soda lake near the Tanzanian border. Drive past Ngong Hills and visit Olorgesaille Archaeological Site. After exploring the site, enjoy a picnic lunch at the lake’s hot springs, observe flamingos, and learn about the Maasai culture from a local guide. Return to Nairobi for dinner and an overnight stay.::

Head to Lake Naivasha, where a boat ride to Crescent Island offers a chance to spot giraffes, hippos, and various birds. After a picnic lunch, spend the rest of the day at leisure and overnight at Crescent Camp.::

Travel to Lake Elementaita and hike the “Sleeping Warrior” hill, a site named after its resemblance to a Maasai warrior. Observe pelicans on the lakeshore before enjoying meals and an overnight stay at Jacaranda Elementaita.::

Continue to Lake Nakuru National Park, home to flamingos, waterbucks, zebras, and rhinos. After lunch at Flamingo Hill Camp, embark on a game drive to observe wildlife. Overnight at Flamingo Hill Camp.::

Visit Lake Bogoria, known for its hot springs, geysers, and large flocks of flamingos. Spot bird species like bee-eaters and kingfishers as well as rare mammals such as the Great Kudu. Overnight at Tumbili Cliff Lodge.::

Discover Lake Baringo, a freshwater lake with over 450 bird species, crocodiles, and hippos. Witness local fishermen on balsa wood boats and enjoy the spectacle of fish eagles hunting. Overnight at Tumbili Cliff Lodge.::

Travel through the Kerio Valley to Iten, the famous “home of marathon runners.” Visit the Chebloch Gorge, observe elephants at Rimoi National Reserve, and take in the breathtaking views from Kerio View Hotel, where you can also try paragliding.:: Rift Valley Wildlife Exploration Turkana and Central Island Experience
Drive to Kitale, located at the foot of Mt. Elgon. Explore the mountain or participate in a coffee tasting at Cranes Camp. Overnight at The Cranes Nested Tented Camp.::

Fly to Lodwar and drive to Eliye Springs on the shores of Lake Turkana. Relax at the beach, visit a local fishing village, and enjoy a freshly prepared meal of lake fish. Overnight at Eliye Springs Resort.::
Take a day trip to Nariokotome, where you can visit the site where the famous “Turkana Boy” skeleton was discovered. Experience the local Turkana culture and explore the area’s history.::

Visit Central Island National Park, located in the middle of Lake Turkana. Explore its volcanic craters, which are breeding grounds for Nile crocodiles, flamingos, and tilapia. Return to Eliye Springs for an overnight stay.::
Spend a leisurely day on the beach with an optional massage or fishing trip. Enjoy a sunset boat cruise for a final safari experience. Overnight at Eliye Springs.::
Fly back to Nairobi or extend your stay at Lake Turkana.::

touren.name: 12 Days Photographic Safari Kenya
touren.desc: This itinerary offers a rich experience of Kenya’s wildlife, landscapes, and culture. Starting in Nairobi with a visit to the bird gallery at the National Museum, you’ll then travel to Lake Naivasha for scenic views and wildlife watching. Next, you’ll explore Lake Bogoria's hot springs and flamingos, and stay at Lake Baringo, famous for its birds and local fishermen. The journey continues to Iten and Kerio Valley, followed by a visit to Kisumu on Lake Victoria’s shores for relaxation or optional activities. The trip ends with an exciting safari in Maasai Mara, where you’ll see wildlife, enjoy game drives, and visit a Maasai village, before heading back to Nairobi. Each day offers nature walks, birdwatching, and other optional activities like boat rides and hot air balloon safaris.
highlights: Game drives and park fees according to the itinerary.
Mineral water during game drives.
We are taking a decisive step toward a sustainable future, where every safari journey contributes to preserving the environment for future generations.
itinerary: Arrival in Nairobi:: Upon arrival at the airport, you’ll be greeted by a representative and transferred to Trademark Hotel for your stay. Later in the day, you’ll visit the National Museum’s bird gallery, offering a glimpse of Kenya’s endemic bird species. The day concludes with an early dinner at a restaurant, followed by a return to the hotel for overnight rest.

After breakfast, you’ll embark on a scenic drive via the Great North Road to Lake Naivasha, with panoramic stops at viewpoints to admire Mt. Suswa and Mt. Longonot. On arrival mid-morning, you’ll walk along the lake shores, home to over 450 bird species. After lunch at your lodge or camp, you can opt for a boat ride on the lake or a visit to Hell’s Gate National Park. Hell’s Gate, known for geothermal activity, offers dramatic landscapes, biking, rock climbing, and wildlife viewing.

Lake Naivasha to Lake Bogoria and Baringo:: The day begins with a scenic drive through the northern Rift Valley towards Lake Bogoria. A picnic lunch is followed by a walk along Lake Bogoria’s shores, where you’ll see its famed hot springs and geysers. The lake is also home to millions of flamingos. Later, you’ll head to Lake Baringo, arriving in the evening for dinner and overnight at Tumbili Cliff Lodge.

Lake Baringo:: After breakfast, enjoy breathtaking views of Lake Baringo and its islands. You’ll have the opportunity to observe over 450 bird species, as well as crocodiles and hippos. Local fishermen in balsa wood boats fish for Tilapia, and you may witness an African Fish Eagle catch a fish in a special spectacle. Dinner and overnight will be at Tumbili Cliff Lodge.

Lake Baringo to Iten:: Departing Lake Baringo after breakfast, you’ll take a scenic route toward Iten, famous for its Olympic champion athletes. Along the way, you’ll stop at Cheploch Gorge for photo opportunities. You’ll arrive at Kerio View Hotel in Iten for lunch, with dinner and overnight at the hotel.

Iten to Kisumu:: After breakfast, visit the training grounds for Kenya’s elite athletes before heading to Kisumu, a Millennium City. Enjoy a relaxing lunch and evening by Lake Victoria’s shores, with dinner and overnight at The Sovereign Hotel.

Kisumu and Surrounding:: Today, you have the option to visit Kakamega Forest for a nature excursion or travel to Rusinga Island for a quiet day on the shores of Lake Victoria. Dinner and overnight will be at The Sovereign Hotel.
Lake Victoria:: Spend the day relaxing by Lake Victoria’s shores or partake in optional water activities like island hopping. Dinner and overnight will be at The Sovereign Hotel.

Kisumu to Maasai Mara:: After breakfast, you’ll travel to Maasai Mara Game Reserve, famous for its wildlife and the Great Migration. En route to your lodge, you’ll enjoy a game drive before lunch. Later in the evening, take another game drive to explore the Mara’s diverse wildlife. Dinner and overnight at Basecamp.

Maasai Mara to Nairobi:: After a final morning game drive and breakfast, you’ll drive back to Nairobi, arriving in time for a late lunch. You’ll then be transferred to the airport for your departure flight.

touren.name: 8 Days Kenya Horizon
touren.desc: In the evening, enjoy a scenic game drive where you’ll encounter various wildlife species such as lions, elephants, and zebras amidst breathtaking savannah landscapes.
highlights: You’ll check in at the Trademark Hotel, where you can relax after the flight and prepare for your safari.
Upon arrival at Flamingo Hills Camp, you’ll check in and enjoy a lunch.
In the early evening, you’ll go on a game drive where you’ll witness wildlife such as waterbucks, zebras, and the park’s famous flamingos.
itinerary: Nairobi:: Upon arrival at Nairobi’s airport, you will be greeted by a personal guide who will transfer you to your hotel. The journey through the city center allows you to witness the vibrant life of Kenya’s capital. You’ll check in at the Trademark Hotel, where you can relax after the flight and prepare for your safari. Your stay includes bed and breakfast (BB).

Lake Nakuru National Park:: After breakfast, you’ll embark on a 3.5-hour drive to Lake Nakuru National Park. Upon arrival at Flamingo Hills Camp, you’ll check in and enjoy a lunch. In the early evening, you’ll go on a game drive where you’ll witness wildlife such as waterbucks, zebras, and the park’s famous flamingos. The park is a sanctuary for endangered species like black rhinos and Rothschild giraffes. You’ll learn about conservation efforts to protect the dwindling rhino population. After an exciting day, you’ll return to Flamingo Hill Camp for dinner and an overnight stay.

Masai Mara:: Depart Lake Nakuru and drive to the iconic Masai Mara Game Reserve. Known for its abundance of wildlife, Masai Mara is the northern extension of the Serengeti-Mara ecosystem, famous for the annual wildebeest migration. On arrival at the Basecamp lodge, you’ll check in and have lunch. In the evening, enjoy a scenic game drive where you’ll encounter various wildlife species such as lions, elephants, and zebras amidst breathtaking savannah landscapes. All meals and an overnight stay will be at Basecamp.

Masai Mara:: Spend the day exploring Masai Mara, a wildlife haven teeming with elephants, giraffes, gazelles, and over 450 bird species. Optional activities include a hot air balloon ride over the Mara plains, followed by a champagne breakfast or a visit to a Maasai village to experience local culture (both available at extra cost). All meals and an overnight stay are again at Basecamp.

Lake Naivasha:: After breakfast, drive to Lake Naivasha, a serene freshwater lake located in the Great Rift Valley. Lake Naivasha, surrounded by the dramatic backdrop of Mount Longonot, was historically a landing site for East Africa’s first air passengers. After checking in and having lunch, you’ll take a peaceful afternoon boat ride, offering opportunities to spot numerous bird species and admire the area’s natural beauty. Dinner and overnight stay will be at Lake Naivasha Country Club.
to Day 5:: Masai Mara and Lake Naivasha
to Day 8:: Amboseli National Park and Departure

Amboseli National Park:: In the morning, drive to Amboseli National Park, known for its iconic views of Mt. Kilimanjaro. The park’s dry and dusty landscape contrasts with lush green areas created by underground streams from Kilimanjaro’s melting snow. You’ll spot big game such as elephants, giraffes, zebras, and buffaloes. After a thrilling day of game viewing, dinner and an overnight stay will be at Kilima Camp.

Amboseli National Park:: Spend another full day in Amboseli, exploring both in the morning and afternoon. Besides elephants, you’ll encounter lions, cheetahs, and a variety of bird species like the African fish eagle and pygmy falcon. This is also a great day for photographers, as the clear views of Mt. Kilimanjaro and its towering presence provide stunning backdrops. Dinner and overnight stay will be at Kilima Camp.

Departure from Nairobi:: After breakfast, depart Amboseli for the drive back to Nairobi. Depending on your flight schedule, you’ll be dropped off at the airport or continue to a beach holiday (optional activities and meals at extra cost).
